General guidelines.
   - When you execute commands, always save their logs to files for easier reading if the output becomes large.
   - When running commands, do not set a timeout yourself — let them run as long as needed (default timeout - 2 minutes is more than enough), and once they finish, review the logs in the file.
   - When running sudo commands (especially package installations like apt-get, yum, npm install, etc.), always run them in the background to avoid timeout issues and permission errors when the process needs to be killed. Use the run_in_background parameter or append & to the command.
   - When CI is failing or user reports failures, consider adding a detailed investigation protocol to your todo list with these steps:
      Step 1: List recent runs with timestamps using: gh run list --repo link-assistant/hive-mind --branch issue-1083-557ffb192515 --limit 5 --json databaseId,conclusion,createdAt,headSha
      Step 2: Verify runs are after the latest commit by checking timestamps and SHA
      Step 3: For each non-passing run, download logs to preserve them: gh run view {run-id} --repo link-assistant/hive-mind --log > ci-logs/{workflow}-{run-id}.log
      Step 4: Read each downloaded log file using Read tool to understand the actual failures
      Step 5: Report findings with specific errors and line numbers from logs
      This detailed investigation is especially helpful when user mentions CI failures, asks to investigate logs, you see non-passing status, or when finalizing a PR.
      Note: If user says \"failing\" but tools show \"passing\", this might indicate stale data - consider downloading fresh logs and checking timestamps to resolve the discrepancy.
   - When a code or log file has more than 1500 lines, read it in chunks of 1500 lines.
   - When facing a complex problem, do as much tracing as possible and turn on all verbose modes.
   - When you create debug, test, or example/experiment scripts for fixing, always keep them in an examples and/or experiments folders so you can reuse them later.
   - When testing your assumptions, use the experiment scripts, and add it to experiments folder.
   - When your experiments can show real world use case of the software, add it to examples folder.
   - When you face something extremely hard, use divide and conquer — it always helps.

Function that returns the current log path\n * @param {Function} log - Logging function\n * @param {Function} cleanup - Optional cleanup function to call on exit\n */\nexport const initializeExitHandler = (getLogPath, log, cleanup = null) => {\n  getLogPathFunction = getLogPath;\n  logFunction = log;\n  cleanupFunction = cleanup;\n};\n\n/**\n * Display the exit message with log path\n */\nconst showExitMessage = async (reason = 'Process exiting', code = 0) => {\n  if (exitMessageShown || !getLogPathFunction || !logFunction) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  exitMessageShown = true;\n\n  // Get the current log path dynamically\n  const currentLogPath = await getLogPathFunction();\n\n  // Always show the log path on exit\n  await logFunction('');\n  if (code === 0) {\n    await logFunction(`✅ ${reason}`);\n  } else {\n    await logFunction(`❌ ${reason}`, { level: 'error' });\n  }\n  await logFunction(`📁 Full log file: ${currentLogPath}`);\n};\n\n/**\n * Safe exit function that ensures log path is shown\n */\nexport const safeExit = async (code = 0, reason = 'Process completed') => {\n  await showExitMessage(reason, code);\n\n  // Close Sentry to flush any pending events and allow the process to exit cleanly\n  try {\n    const sentry = await getSentry();\n    if (sentry && sentry.close) {\n      await sentry.close(2000); // Wait up to 2 seconds for pending events to be sent\n    }\n  } catch {\n    // Ignore Sentry.close() errors - exit anyway\n  }\n\n  process.exit(code);\n};\n\n/**\n * Install global exit handlers to ensure log path is always shown\n */\nexport const installGlobalExitHandlers = () => {\n  // Handle normal exit\n  process.on('exit', code => {\n    // Synchronous fallback - [2026-01-09T23:54:51.334Z] [INFO] it sentry.close(2000);\n      }\n    } catch {\n      // Ignore Sentry.close() errors\n    }\n    process.exit(143);\n  });\n\n  // Handle uncaught exceptions\n  process.on('uncaughtException', async error => {\n    if (cleanupFunction) {\n      try {\n        await cleanupFunction();\n      } catch {\n        // Ignore cleanup errors on exception\n      }\n    }\n    if (logFunction) {\n      await logFunction(`\\n❌ Uncaught Exception: ${error.message}`, { level: 'error' });\n    }\n    await showExitMessage('Uncaught exception occurred', 1);\n    try {\n      const sentry = await getSentry();\n      if (sentry && sentry.close) {\n        await sentry.close(2000);\n      }\n    } catch {\n      // Ignore Sentry.close() errors\n    }\n    process.exit(1);\n  });\n\n  // Handle unhandled rejections\n  process.on('unhandledRejection', async reason => {\n    if (cleanupFunction) {\n      try {\n        await cleanupFunction();\n      } catch {\n        // Ignore cleanup errors on rejection\n      }\n    }\n    if (logFunction) {\n      await logFunction(`\\n❌ Unhandled Rejection: ${reason}`, { level: 'error' });\n    }\n    await showExitMessage('Unhandled rejection occurred', 1);\n    try {\n      const sentry = await getSentry();\n      if (sentry && sentry.close) {\n        await sentry.close(2000);\n      }\n    } catch {\n      // Ignore Sentry.close() errors\n    }\n    process.exit(1);\n  });\n};\n\n/**\n * Reset the exit message flag (useful for testing)\n */\nexport const resetExitHandler = () => {\n  exitMessageShown = false;\n};\n","numLines":206,"startLine":1,"totalLines":206}}}
[2026-01-09T23:54:51.937Z] [INFO] {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01LbCgnSk2kD6htosBuBzb1M","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→#!/usr/bin/env node\n 17→\n    18→import { exec } from 'node:child_process';\n    19→import { promisify } from 'node:util';\n    20→\n    21→const execAsync = promisify(exec);\n    22→\n    23→// Import centralized limits 27→ * Configuration constants for queue throttling\n    28→ * All thresholds use ratios (0.0 - 1.0) representing usage percentage\n    29→ *\n    30→ * IMPORTANT: Running claude processes is NOT a blocking limit by itself.\n    31→ * Commands can run in parallel as long as actual limits (CPU, API, etc.) are not exceeded.\n    32→ * See: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1078\n    33→ */\n    34→export const QUEUE_CONFIG = {\n    35→  // Resource thresholds (usage ratios: 0.0 - 1.0)\n    36→  RAM_THRESHOLD: 0.5, // Stop if RAM usage > 50%\n    37→  // CPU threshold uses 5-minute load average, not instantaneous CPU usage\n    38→  CPU_THRESHOLD: 0.5, // Stop if 5-minute load average > 50% of CPU count\n    39→  DISK_THRESHOLD: 0.95, // One-at-a-time if disk usage > 95%\n    40→\n    41→  // API limit thresholds (usage ratios: 0.0 - 1.0)\n    42→  CLAUDE_SESSION_THRESHOLD: 0.9, // Stop if 5-hour limit > 90%\n    43→  CLAUDE_WEEKLY_THRESHOLD: 0.99, // One-at-a-time if weekly limit > 99%\n    44→  GITHUB_API_THRESHOLD: 0.8, // Stop if GitHub > 80% with parallel claude\n    45→\n    46→  // Timing\n    47→  // MIN_START_INTERVAL_MS: Time to allow solve command to start actual claude process\n    48→  // This ensures that when API limits are checked, the running process is counted\n    49→  MIN_START_INTERVAL_MS: 120000, // 2 minutes between */\n    60→export const QueueItemStatus = {\n    61→  QUEUED: 'queued',\n    62→  WAITING: 'waiting',\n    63→  STARTING: 'starting',\n    64→  STARTED: 'started',\n    65→  FAILED: 'failed',\n    66→  CANCELLED: 'cancelled',\n    67→};\n    68→\n    69→/**\n    70→ * Count running claude processes\n    71→ * @param {boolean} verbose - Whether to log verbose output\n    72→ * @returns execAsync('pgrep -l -x claude 2>/dev/null || true');\n    77→    const lines = stdout\n    78→      .trim()\n    79→      .split('\\n')\n    80→      .filter(line => line.trim());\n    81→\n    82→ }\n    97→    }\n    98→\n    99→    return {\n   100→      count: processes.length,\n   101→      processes: processes.map(p => `${p.pid}:${p.name}`),\n   102→    };\n   103→  } catch (error) {\n   104→    if (verbose) {\n   105→      console.error('[VERBOSE] /solve-queue error counting claude processes:', error.message);\n   106→    }\n   107→    return { count: 0, processes: [] };\n   108→  }\n   109→}\n   110→\n   111→/**\n   112→ * Format a threshold as percentage for display\n   113→ * @param {number} ratio - Ratio (0.0 - 1.0)\n   114→ * @returns {string} Formatted percentage\n   115→ */\n   116→function formatThresholdPercent(ratio) {\n   117→  return `${Math.round(ratio * 100)}%`;\n   118→}\n   119→\n   120→/**\n   121→ * Generate human-readable waiting reason based on threshold violation\n   122→ * @param {string} metric - The metric name (ram, cpu, disk, etc.)\n   123→ * @param {number} currentValue - Current value (as percentage 0-100)\n   124→ * @param {number} threshold - Threshold ratio (0.0 - 1.0)\n   125→ * @returns {string} Human-readable reason\n   126→ */\n   127→function formatWaitingReason(metric, currentValue, threshold) {\n   128→  const thresholdPercent = formatThresholdPercent(threshold);\n   129→  const currentPercent = Math.round(currentValue);\n   130→\n   131→  switch (metric) {\n   132→    case 'ram':\n   133→      return `RAM usage is ${currentPercent}% (threshold: ${thresholdPercent})`;\n   134→    case 'cpu':\n   135→      return `CPU usage is ${currentPercent}% (threshold: ${thresholdPercent})`;\n   136→    case 'disk':\n   137→      return `Disk usage is ${currentPercent}% (threshold: ${thresholdPercent})`;\n   138→    case 'claude_session':\n   139→      return `Claude session limit is ${currentPercent}% (threshold: ${thresholdPercent})`;\n   140→    case 'claude_weekly':\n   141→      return `Claude weekly limit is ${currentPercent}% (threshold: ${thresholdPercent})`;\n   142→    case 'github':\n   143→      return `GitHub API usage is ${currentPercent}% (threshold: ${thresholdPercent})`;\n   144→    case 'min_interval':\n   145→      return `Minimum interval between commands not reached`;\n   146→    case 'claude_running':\n   147→      return `Claude process is already running`;\n   148→    default:\n   149→      return `${metric} threshold exceeded`;\n   150→  }\n   151→}\n   152→\n   153→/**\n   154→ * Queue item representing a /solve command request\n   155→ */\n   156→class SolveQueueItem {\n   157→  constructor(options) {\n   158→    this.id = `solve-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).substring(2, 9)}`;\n   159→    this.url = options.url;\n   160→    this.args = options.args;\n   161→    this.ctx = options.ctx;\n   162→ this.sessionName = null;\n   172→    // Message tracking - forget after STARTED\n   173→    this.messageInfo = null; // { chatId, messageId }\n   174→    // Track when we last updated the Telegram 217→  /**\n   218→   * Mark item as cancelled\n   219→   */\n   220→  setCancelled() {\n   221→    this.status = QueueItemStatus.CANCELLED;\n   222→  }\n   223→\n   224→  /**\n   225→   * Get wait time in queue (ms)\n   226→   */\n   227→  getWaitTime() {\n   228→    const endTime = this.startedAt || new Date();\n   229→    return endTime - this.createdAt;\n   230→  }\n   231→\n   232→  /**\n   233→   * Format for display\n   234→   * @returns {string}\n   235→   */\n   236→  toString() {\n   237→    return `[${this.id}] ${this.url} (${this.status})`;\n   238→  }\n   239→}\n   240→\n   241→/**\n   242→ * Solve Queue - Producer/Consumer queue for /solve commands\n   243→ */\n   244→export class SolveQueue {\n   245→  constructor(options = {}) {\n   246→    this.verbose = // Timing\n   258→    this.lastStartTime = null;\n   259→\n   260→    // Consumer task reference\n   261→    this.consumerTask = null;\n   262→\n   263→    // Statistics\n   264→    this.stats = {\n   265→      totalEnqueued: 0,\n   266→      totalStarted: 0,\n   267→      totalCompleted: 0,\n   268→      totalFailed: 0,\n   269→      totalCancelled: 0,\n   270→      throttleReasons: {},\n   271→    };\n   272→\n   273→    this.log('SolveQueue initialized');\n   274→  }\n   275→\n   276→  /**\n   277→   * Log message if verbose mode is enabled\n   278→   * @param {string} message\n   279→   */\n   280→  log(message) {\n   281→    if (this.verbose) {\n   282→      console.log(`[VERBOSE] /solve-queue: ${message}`);\n   283→    }\n   284→  }\n   285→\n   286→  /**\n   287→   * Add a solve command to the queue\n   288→   * @param {Object} options - Queue item options\n   289→   * @returns {SolveQueueItem} The queued item\n   290→   */\n   291→  enqueue(options) {\n   292→    const item = new SolveQueueItem(options);\n   293→    this.queue.push(item);\n   294→    this.stats.totalEnqueued++;\n   295→\n   296→    this.log(`Enqueued: ${item.toString()}, queue length: ${this.queue.length}`);\n   297→\n   298→    // Start consumer if not already running\n   299→    this.ensureConsumerRunning();\n   300→\n   301→    return item;\n   302→  }\n   303→\n   304→  /**\n   305→   * Cancel a queued item by ID\n   306→   * @param {string} id - Item ID\n   307→   * @returns {boolean} True if cancelled\n   308→   */\n   309→  cancel(id) {\n   310→    const queueIndex = this.queue.findIndex(item => item.id === id);\n   311→    if (queueIndex !== -1) {\n   312→      const item = this.queue.splice(queueIndex, 1)[0];\n   313→      item.setCancelled();\n   314→      this.stats.totalCancelled++;\n   315→      this.log(`Cancelled queued item: ${item.toString()}`);\n   316→      return true;\n   317→    }\n   318→\n   319→    if (this.processing.has(id)) {\n   320→      this.log(`Cannot cancel processing item: ${id}`);\n   321→      return false;\n   322→    }\n   323→\n   324→    return false;\n   325→  }\n   326→\n   327→  /**\n   328→   * Get queue statistics\n   329→   * @returns {Object}\n   330→   */\n   331→  getStats() {\n   332→    return {\n   333→      queued: this.queue.length,\n   334→      processing: this.processing.size,\n   335→      completed: this.completed.length,\n   336→      failed: this.failed.length,\n   337→      ...this.stats,\n   338→      cacheStats: getLimitCache().getStats(),\n   339→      lastStartTime: this.lastStartTime,\n   340→      isRunning: this.isRunning,\n   341→    };\n   342→  }\n   343→\n   344→  /**\n   345→   * Get queue items summary for display\n   346→   * @returns {Object}\n   347→   */\n   348→  getQueueSummary() {\n   349→    return {\n   350→      pending: this.queue.map(item => ({\n   351→        id: item.id,\n   352→        url: item.url,\n   353→        requester: item.requester,\n   354→        waitTime: item.getWaitTime(),\n   355→        createdAt: item.createdAt,\n   356→        status: item.status,\n   357→        waitingReason: item.waitingReason,\n   358→      })),\n   359→      processing: Array.from(this.processing.values()).map(item => ({\n   360→        id: item.id,\n   361→        url: item.url,\n   362→        requester: item.requester,\n   363→        startedAt: item.startedAt,\n   364→        status: item.status,\n   365→      })),\n   366→    };\n   367→  }\n   368→\n   369→  /**\n   370→   * Check if a new command can start\n   371→   *\n   372→   * Logic per issue #1061:\n   373→   * 1. \"Claude process is already running\" is NOT a limit by itself - it's a metric\n   374→   * 2. Commands can run in parallel as long as actual limits are not exceeded\n   375→   * 3. When any limit >= threshold, allow exactly one claude command to pass\n   376→   *\n   377→   * @returns {Promise<{canStart: boolean, reason?: string, reasons?: string[], oneAtATime?: boolean}>}\n   378→   */\n   379→  async canStartCommand() {\n   380→    const reasons = [];\n   381→    let oneAtATime = false;\n   382→\n   383→    // Check minimum interval since last start\n   384→    if (this.lastStartTime) {\n   385→      const timeSinceLastStart = Date.now() - this.lastStartTime;\n   386→      if (timeSinceLastStart < QUEUE_CONFIG.MIN_START_INTERVAL_MS) {\n   387→        const waitSeconds = Math.ceil((QUEUE_CONFIG.MIN_START_INTERVAL_MS - timeSinceLastStart) / 1000);\n   388→        reasons.push(formatWaitingReason('min_interval', 0, 0) + ` (${waitSeconds}s remaining)`);\n   389→        this.recordThrottle('min_interval');\n   390→      }\n   391→    }\n   392→\n   393→    // Check running claude processes (this is a metric, not a blocking reason by itself)\n   394→    const claudeProcs = await getRunningClaudeProcesses(this.verbose);\n   395→    const hasRunningClaude = claudeProcs.count > 0;\n   396→\n   397→    // Track claude_running as a metric (but don't add to reasons yet)\n   398→    if (hasRunningClaude) {\n   399→      this.recordThrottle('claude_running');\n   400→    }\n   401→\n   402→    // Check system resources\n   403→    const resourceCheck = await this.checkSystemResources();\n   404→    if (!resourceCheck.ok) {\n   405→      reasons.push(...resourceCheck.reasons);\n   406→    }\n   407→    if (resourceCheck.oneAtATime) {\n   408→      oneAtATime = true;\n   409→    }\n   410→\n   411→    // Check API limits (pass hasRunningClaude to enable special handling)\n   412→    const limitCheck = await this.checkApiLimits(hasRunningClaude);\n   413→    if (!limitCheck.ok) {\n   414→      reasons.push(...limitCheck.reasons);\n   415→    }\n   416→    if (limitCheck.oneAtATime) {\n   417→      oneAtATime = true;\n   418→    }\n   419→\n   420→    // \"Claude process running\" only blocks if there are OTHER reasons too\n   421→    // This allows parallel execution when limits are not exceeded\n   422→    if (hasRunningClaude && reasons.length > 0) {\n   423→      // Add claude_running info at the END (not beginning) of reasons\n   424→      // Since it's supplementary info, not the primary blocking reason\n   425→      // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1078\n   426→      reasons.push(formatWaitingReason('claude_running', claudeProcs.count, 0) + ` (${claudeProcs.count} processes)`);\n   427→    }\n   428→\n   429→    const canStart = reasons.length === 0;\n   430→\n   431→    if (!canStart && this.verbose) {\n   432→      this.log(`Cannot start: ${reasons.join(', ')}`);\n   433→    }\n   434→\n   435→    return {\n   436→      canStart,\n   437→      reason: reasons.length > 0 ? reasons.join('\\n') : undefined,\n   438→      reasons,\n   439→      oneAtATime,\n   440→      claudeProcesses: claudeProcs.count,\n   441→    };\n   442→  }\n   443→\n   444→  /**\n   445→   * Check system resources (RAM, CPU, disk) using cached values\n   446→   *\n   447→   * Uses 5-minute load average for CPU instead of instantaneous usage.\n   448→   * This provides a more stable metric that isn't affected by brief spikes\n   449→   * during claude process startup.\n   450→   *\n   451→   * @returns {Promise<{ok: boolean, reasons: string[], oneAtATime: boolean}>}\n   452→   */\n   453→  async checkSystemResources() {\n   454→    const reasons = [];\n   455→    let oneAtATime = false;\n   456→\n   457→    // Check RAM (using cached value)\n   458→    const memResult = await getCachedMemoryInfo(this.verbose);\n   459→    if (memResult.success) {\n   460→      const usedRatio = memResult.memory.usedPercentage / 100;\n   461→      if (usedRatio > QUEUE_CONFIG.RAM_THRESHOLD) {\n   462→        reasons.push(formatWaitingReason('ram', memResult.memory.usedPercentage, QUEUE_CONFIG.RAM_THRESHOLD));\n   463→        this.recordThrottle('ram_high');\n   464→      }\n   465→    }\n   466→\n   467→    // Check CPU using 5-minute load average (more stable than 1-minute)\n   468→    // Cache TTL is 2 minutes, which is appropriate for this metric\n   469→    const cpuResult = await getCachedCpuInfo(this.verbose);\n   470→    if (cpuResult.success) {\n   471→      // Use loadAvg5 (5-minute average) instead of usagePercentage (1-minute based)\n   472→      // This provides a more stable metric that isn't affected by transient spikes\n   473→      const loadAvg5 = cpuResult.cpuLoad.loadAvg5;\n   474→      const cpuCount = cpuResult.cpuLoad.cpuCount;\n   475→      // Calculate usage ratio: loadAvg5 / cpuCount\n   476→      // Load average of 1.0 per CPU = 100% utilization\n   477→      const usageRatio = loadAvg5 / cpuCount;\n   478→      const usagePercent = Math.min(100, Math.round(usageRatio * 100));\n   479→\n   480→      if (this.verbose) {\n   481→        this.log(`CPU 5m load avg: ${loadAvg5.toFixed(2)}, cpus: ${cpuCount}, usage: ${usagePercent}%`);\n   482→      }\n   483→\n   484→      if (usageRatio > QUEUE_CONFIG.CPU_THRESHOLD) {\n   485→        reasons.push(formatWaitingReason('cpu', usagePercent, QUEUE_CONFIG.CPU_THRESHOLD));\n   486→        this.recordThrottle('cpu_high');\n   487→      }\n   488→    }\n   489→\n   490→    // Check disk space (using cached value)\n   491→    const diskResult = await getCachedDiskInfo(this.verbose);\n   492→    if (diskResult.success) {\n   493→      // Calculate usage from free percentage\n   494→      const usedPercent = 100 - diskResult.diskSpace.freePercentage;\n   495→      const usedRatio = usedPercent / 100;\n   496→      if (usedRatio > QUEUE_CONFIG.DISK_THRESHOLD) {\n   497→        oneAtATime = true;\n   498→        this.recordThrottle('disk_high');\n   499→        if (this.processing.size > 0) {\n   500→          reasons.push(formatWaitingReason('disk', usedPercent, QUEUE_CONFIG.DISK_THRESHOLD) + ' (waiting for current command)');\n   501→        }\n   502→      }\n   503→    }\n   504→\n   505→    return { ok: reasons.length === 0, reasons, oneAtATime };\n   506→  }\n   507→\n   508→  /**\n   509→   * Check API limits (Claude, GitHub) using cached values\n   510→   *\n   511→   * Simplified logic per issue #1061:\n   512→   * - When any limit >= threshold, allow exactly one claude command to pass\n   513→   * - Only block if there's already a command in progress\n   514→   * - Running claude is the ultimate test of whether limits are really exhausted\n   515→   *\n   516→   * @param {boolean} hasRunningClaude - Whether claude processes are running\n   517→   * @returns {Promise<{ok: boolean, reasons: string[], oneAtATime: boolean}>}\n   518→   */\n   519→  async checkApiLimits(hasRunningClaude = false) {\n   520→    const reasons = [];\n   521→    let oneAtATime = false;\n   522→\n   523→    // Check Claude limits (using cached value)\n   524→    const claudeResult = await getCachedClaudeLimits(this.verbose);\n   525→    if (claudeResult.success) {\n   526→      const sessionPercent = claudeResult.usage.currentSession.percentage;\n   527→      const weeklyPercent = claudeResult.usage.allModels.percentage;\n   528→\n   529→      // Session limit (5-hour)\n   530→      // When above threshold: allow exactly one command, block if one is running\n   531→      if (sessionPercent !== null) {\n   532→        const sessionRatio = sessionPercent / 100;\n   533→        if (sessionRatio >= QUEUE_CONFIG.CLAUDE_SESSION_THRESHOLD) {\n   534→          // Only block if Claude is already running\n   535→          if (hasRunningClaude) {\n   536→            reasons.push(formatWaitingReason('claude_session', sessionPercent, QUEUE_CONFIG.CLAUDE_SESSION_THRESHOLD));\n   537→          }\n   538→          this.recordThrottle(sessionRatio >= 1.0 ? 'claude_session_100' : 'claude_session_high');\n   539→        }\n   540→      }\n   541→\n   542→      // Weekly limit\n   543→      // When above threshold: allow exactly one command, block if one is in progress\n   544→      if (weeklyPercent !== null) {\n   545→        const weeklyRatio = weeklyPercent / 100;\n   546→        if (weeklyRatio >= QUEUE_CONFIG.CLAUDE_WEEKLY_THRESHOLD) {\n   547→          oneAtATime = true;\n   548→          this.recordThrottle(weeklyRatio >= 1.0 ? 'claude_weekly_100' : 'claude_weekly_high');\n   549→          // Only block if command is already in progress\n   550→          if (this.processing.size > 0) {\n   551→            reasons.push(formatWaitingReason('claude_weekly', weeklyPercent, QUEUE_CONFIG.CLAUDE_WEEKLY_THRESHOLD) + ' (waiting for current command)');\n   552→          }\n   553→        }\n   554→      }\n   555→    }\n   556→\n   557→    // Check GitHub limits (only relevant if claude processes running)\n   558→    if (hasRunningClaude) {\n   559→      const githubResult = await getCachedGitHubLimits(this.verbose);\n   560→      if (githubResult.success) {\n   561→        const usedPercent = githubResult.githubRateLimit.usedPercentage;\n   562→        const usedRatio = usedPercent / 100;\n   563→        if (usedRatio >= QUEUE_CONFIG.GITHUB_API_THRESHOLD) {\n   564→          reasons.push(formatWaitingReason('github', usedPercent, QUEUE_CONFIG.GITHUB_API_THRESHOLD));\n   565→          this.recordThrottle(usedRatio >= 1.0 ? 'github_100' : 'github_high');\n   566→        }\n   567→      }\n   568→    }\n   569→\n   570→    return { ok: reasons.length === 0, reasons, oneAtATime };\n   571→  }\n   572→\n   573→  /**\n   574→   * Record a throttle event for statistics\n   575→   * @param {string} reason\n   576→   */\n   577→  recordThrottle(reason) {\n   578→    this.stats.throttleReasons[reason] = (this.stats.throttleReasons[reason] || 0) + 1;\n   579→  }\n   580→\n   581→  /**\n   582→   * Ensure consumer task is running\n   583→   */\n   584→  ensureConsumerRunning() {\n   585→    if (this.consumerTask) return;\n   586→\n   587→    this.consumerTask = this.runConsumer();\n   588→    this.consumerTask.catch(error => {\n   589→      console.error('[solve-queue] Consumer error:', error);\n   590→      this.consumerTask = null;\n   591→    });\n   592→  }\n   593→\n   594→  /**\n   595→   * Update item message in Telegram\n   596→   * @param {SolveQueueItem} item\n   597→   * @param {string} text\n   598→   * @param {boolean} trackUpdateTime - Whether to track this as a periodic update (default: true)\n   599→   */\n   600→  async updateItemMessage(item, text, trackUpdateTime = true) {\n   601→    if (!item.messageInfo || !item.ctx) return;\n   602→\n   603→    try {\n   604→      const { chatId, messageId } = item.messageInfo;\n   605→      await item.ctx.telegram.editMessageText(chatId, messageId, undefined, text, { parse_mode: 'Markdown' });\n   606→      if (trackUpdateTime) {\n   607→        item.lastMessageUpdateTime = Date.now();\n   608→      }\n   609→    } catch (error) {\n   610→      this.log(`Failed to update message: ${error.message}`);\n   611→    }\n   612→  }\n   613→\n   614→  /**\n   615→   * Check if an item's message should be updated periodically\n   616→   * @param {SolveQueueItem} item\n   617→   * @returns {boolean}\n   618→   */\n   619→  shouldUpdateMessage(item) {\n   620→    if (!item.messageInfo || !item.ctx) return false;\n   621→    if (!item.lastMessageUpdateTime) return true; // Never updated\n   622→    return Date.now() - item.lastMessageUpdateTime >= QUEUE_CONFIG.MESSAGE_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS;\n   623→  }\n   624→\n   625→  /**\n   626→   * Consumer loop - processes items from the queue\n   627→   */\n   628→  async runConsumer() {\n   629→    this.log('Consumer started');\n   630→\n   631→    while (this.isRunning) {\n   632→      if (this.queue.length === 0) {\n   633→        await this.sleep(QUEUE_CONFIG.CONSUMER_POLL_INTERVAL_MS);\n   634→        continue;\n   635→      }\n   636→\n   637→      const check = await this.canStartCommand();\n   638→\n   639→      if (!check.canStart) {\n   640→        // Update all queued items to waiting status with reason\n   641→        // Also periodically refresh messages to show current status\n   642→        // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1078\n   643→        for (const item of this.queue) {\n   644→          if (item.status === QueueItemStatus.QUEUED || item.status === QueueItemStatus.WAITING) {\n   645→            const previousStatus = item.status;\n   646→            const previousReason = item.waitingReason;\n   647→            item.setWaiting(check.reason);\n   648→\n   649→            // Update message if:\n   650→            // 1. Status or reason changed\n   651→            // 2. OR it's time for a periodic update (every MESSAGE_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS)\n   652→            const shouldUpdate = previousStatus !== item.status || previousReason !== item.waitingReason || this.shouldUpdateMessage(item);\n   653→\n   654→            if (shouldUpdate) {\n   655→              const position = this.queue.indexOf(item) + 1;\n   656→              await this.updateItemMessage(item, `⏳ Waiting (position #${position})\\n\\n${item.infoBlock}\\n\\n*Reason:*\\n${check.reason}`);\n   657→            }\n   658→          }\n   659→        }\n   660→\n   661→        this.log(`Throttled: ${check.reason}`);\n   662→        await this.sleep(QUEUE_CONFIG.CONSUMER_POLL_INTERVAL_MS);\n   663→        continue;\n   664→      }\n   665→\n   666→      // Check one-at-a-time mode\n   667→      if (check.oneAtATime && this.processing.size > 0) {\n   668→        this.log('One-at-a-time mode: waiting for current command to finish');\n   669→        await this.sleep(QUEUE_CONFIG.CONSUMER_POLL_INTERVAL_MS);\n   670→        continue;\n   671→      }\n   672→\n   673→      // Get next item from queue\n   674→      const item = this.queue.shift();\n   675→      if (!item) continue;\n   676→\n   677→      // NOTE: Running claude processes is NOT a blocking limit by itself\n   678→      // Commands can run in parallel as long as actual limits (CPU, API, etc.) are not exceeded\n   679→      // The MIN_START_INTERVAL_MS ensures enough time for processes to be counted\n   680→      // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1078\n   681→\n   682→      // Update status to Starting\n   683→      item.setStarting();\n   684→      this.processing.set(item.id, item);\n   685→      this.lastStartTime = Date.now();\n   686→      this.stats.totalStarted++;\n   687→\n   688→      // Update message to show Starting status\n   689→      await this.updateItemMessage(item, `🚀 Starting solve command...\\n\\n${item.infoBlock}`);\n   690→\n   691→      this.log(`Starting: ${item.toString()}`);\n   692→\n   693→      // Execute in background\n   694→      this.executeItem(item).catch(error => {\n   695→        console.error(`[solve-queue] Execution error for ${item.id}:`, error);\n   696→      });\n   697→    }\n   698→\n   699→    this.log('Consumer stopped');\n   700→    this.consumerTask = null;\n   701→  }\n   702→\n   703→  /**\n   704→   * Execute a queue item\n   705→   * @param {SolveQueueItem} item\n   706→   */\n   707→  async executeItem(item) {\n   708→    try {\n   709→      if (this.executeCallback) {\n   710→        const result = await this.executeCallback(item);\n   711→\n   712→        // Extract session name from result\n   713→        let sessionName = 'unknown';\n   714→        if (result && result.output) {\n   715→          const sessionMatch = result.output.match(/session:\\s*(\\S+)/i) || result.output.match(/screen -r\\s+(\\S+)/);\n   716→          if (sessionMatch) sessionName = sessionMatch[1];\n   717→        }\n   718→\n   719→        // IMPORTANT: Save messageInfo BEFORE calling setStarted, because setStarted clears it\n   720→        // This was a bug where the final message update never happened because messageInfo was null\n   721→        // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1062\n   722→        const savedMessageInfo = item.messageInfo;\n   723→\n   724→        // Update to Started status (terminal - forgets message tracking)\n   725→        item.setStarted(sessionName);\n   726→        this.stats.totalCompleted++;\n   727→\n   728→        // Final message update using saved messageInfo\n   729→        if (item.ctx && result && savedMessageInfo) {\n   730→          const { chatId, messageId } = savedMessageInfo;\n   731→          if (chatId && messageId) {\n   732→            try {\n   733→              if (result.warning) {\n   734→                await item.ctx.telegram.editMessageText(chatId, messageId, undefined, `⚠️ ${result.warning}`, { parse_mode: 'Markdown' });\n   735→              } else if (result.success) {\n   736→                const response = `✅ Solve command started successfully!\\n\\n📊 Session: \\`${sessionName}\\`\\n\\n${item.infoBlock}`;\n   737→                await item.ctx.telegram.editMessageText(chatId, messageId, undefined, response, { parse_mode: 'Markdown' });\n   738→              } else {\n   739→                const response = `❌ Error executing solve command:\\n\\n\\`\\`\\`\\n${result.error || result.output}\\n\\`\\`\\``;\n   740→                await item.ctx.telegram.editMessageText(chatId, messageId, undefined, response, { parse_mode: 'Markdown' });\n   741→              }\n   742→            } catch (error) {\n   743→              // Log message edit failures for debugging\n   744→              // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1062\n   745→              console.error(`[solve-queue] Failed to update message for item ${item.id}: ${error.message}`);\n   746→            }\n   747→          }\n   748→        }\n   749→      } else {\n   750→        item.setStarted('no-callback');\n   751→        this.stats.totalCompleted++;\n   752→      }\n   753→    } catch (error) {\n   754→      item.setFailed(error);\n   755→      this.stats.totalFailed++;\n   756→      console.error(`[solve-queue] Item failed: ${item.id}`, error);\n   757→\n   758→      // Try to update message with error\n   759→      const { chatId, messageId } = item.messageInfo || {};\n   760→      if (chatId && messageId && item.ctx) {\n   761→        try {\n   762→          await item.ctx.telegram.editMessageText(chatId, messageId, undefined, `❌ Error: ${error.message}`, { parse_mode: 'Markdown' });\n   763→        } catch (editError) {\n   764→          // Log the edit failure for debugging\n   765→          // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1062\n   766→          console.error(`[solve-queue] Failed to update error message for item ${item.id}: ${editError.message}`);\n   767→        }\n   768→      }\n   769→    } finally {\n   770→      this.processing.delete(item.id);\n   771→\n   772→      if (item.status === QueueItemStatus.STARTED) {\n   773→        this.completed.push(item);\n   774→      } else if (item.status === QueueItemStatus.FAILED) {\n   775→        this.failed.push(item);\n   776→      }\n   777→\n   778→      this.log(`Finished: ${item.toString()}`);\n   779→\n   780→      // Limit history size\n   781→      while (this.completed.length > 100) this.completed.shift();\n   782→      while (this.failed.length > 100) this.failed.shift();\n   783→    }\n   784→  }\n   785→\n   786→  /**\n   787→   * Sleep for specified milliseconds\n   788→   * @param {number} ms\n   789→   * @returns {Promise<void>}\n   790→   */\n   791→  sleep(ms) {\n   792→    return new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, ms));\n   793→  }\n   794→\n   795→  /**\n   796→   * Stop the queue\n   797→   */\n   798→  stop() {\n   799→    this.log('Stopping queue...');\n   800→    this.isRunning = false;\n   801→  }\n   802→\n   803→  /**\n   804→   * Clear the limit cache\n   805→   */\n   806→  clearCache() {\n   807→    getLimitCache().clear();\n   808→    this.log('Limit cache cleared');\n   809→  }\n   810→\n   811→  /**\n   812→   * Format queue status for display\n   813→   * @returns {string}\n   814→   */\n   815→  formatStatus() {\n   816→    const stats = this.getStats();\n   817→    if (stats.queued > 0 || stats.processing > 0) {\n   818→      return `Solve Queue: ${stats.queued} pending, ${stats.processing} processing\\n`;\n   819→    }\n   820→    return 'Solve Queue: empty\\n';\n   821→  }\n   822→\n   823→  /**\n   824→   * Format detailed queue status for Telegram message\n   825→   * @returns {string}\n   826→   */\n   827→  formatDetailedStatus() {\n   828→    const stats = this.getStats();\n   829→    const summary = this.getQueueSummary();\n   830→\n   831→    let message = '📋 *Solve Queue Status*\\n\\n';\n   832→    message += `Pending: ${stats.queued}\\n`;\n   833→    message += `Processing: ${stats.processing}\\n`;\n   834→    message += `Completed: ${stats.completed}\\n`;\n   835→    message += `Failed: ${stats.failed}\\n\\n`;\n   836→\n   837→    if (summary.processing.length > 0) {\n   838→      message += '*Currently Processing:*\\n';\n   839→      for (const item of summary.processing) {\n   840→        message += `• ${item.url}\\n`;\n   841→      }\n   842→      message += '\\n';\n   843→    }\n   844→\n   845→    if (summary.pending.length > 0) {\n   846→      message += '*Waiting in Queue:*\\n';\n   847→      for (const item of summary.pending.slice(0, 5)) {\n   848→        const waitSeconds = Math.floor(item.waitTime / 1000);\n   849→        message += `• ${item.url} (${item.status}, ${waitSeconds}s)\\n`;\n   850→        if (item.waitingReason) {\n   851→          message += `  └ ${item.waitingReason}\\n`;\n   852→        }\n   853→      }\n   854→      if (summary.pending.length > 5) {\n   855→        message += `  ... and ${summary.pending.length - 5} more\\n`;\n   856→      }\n   857→    }\n   858→\n   859→    return message;\n   860→  }\n   861→}\n   862→\n   863→/**\n   864→ * Global queue instance (singleton)\n   865→ */\n   866→let globalQueue = null;\n   867→\n   868→/**\n   869→ * Get or create the global solve queue instance\n   870→ * @param {Object} options - Queue options\n   871→ * @returns {SolveQueue}\n   872→ */\n   873→export function getSolveQueue(options = {}) {\n   874→  if (!globalQueue) {\n   875→    globalQueue = new SolveQueue(options);\n   876→  } else if (options.verbose !== undefined) {\n   877→    globalQueue.verbose = options.verbose;\n   878→  }\n   879→  return globalQueue;\n   880→}\n   881→\n   882→/**\n   883→ * Reset the global queue (useful for testing)\n   884→ */\n   885→export function resetSolveQueue() {\n   886→  if (globalQueue) {\n   887→    globalQueue.stop();\n   888→    globalQueue = null;\n   889→  }\n   890→}\n   891→\n   892→/**\n   893→ * Create an execute callback for the queue\n   894→ * @param {Function} executeStartScreen - Function to execute start-screen command\n   895→ * @returns {Function} Execute callback for queue items\n   896→ */\n   897→export function createQueueExecuteCallback(executeStartScreen) {\n   898→  return async item => {\n   899→    return await executeStartScreen('solve', item.args);\n   900→  };\n   901→}\n   902→\n   903→export default {\n   904→  SolveQueue,\n   905→  SolveQueueItem,\n   906→  getSolveQueue,\n   907→  resetSolveQueue,\n Resource checking (RAM, CPU, disk)\n * - API limit checking (Claude, GitHub)\n * - Minimum interval between command starts\n * - Running process detection\n * - Status tracking: Queued -> Waiting -> Starting -> Started\n *\n * @see https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1041\n */\n\nimport { exec } from 'node:child_process';\nimport { promisify } from 'node:util';\n\nconst execAsync = promisify(exec);\n\n// Import centralized limits and caching\nimport { getCachedClaudeLimits, getCachedGitHubLimits, getCachedMemoryInfo, getCachedCpuInfo, getCachedDiskInfo, getLimitCache } from './limits.lib.mjs';\n\n/**\n * Configuration constants for queue throttling\n * All thresholds use ratios (0.0 - 1.0) representing usage percentage\n *\n * IMPORTANT: Running claude processes is NOT a blocking limit by itself.\n * Commands can run in parallel as long as actual limits (CPU, API, etc.) are not exceeded.\n * See: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1078\n */\nexport const QUEUE_CONFIG = {\n  // Resource thresholds (usage ratios: 0.0 - 1.0)\n  RAM_THRESHOLD: 0.5, // Stop if RAM usage > 50%\n  // CPU threshold uses 5-minute load average, not instantaneous CPU usage\n  CPU_THRESHOLD: 0.5, // Stop if 5-minute load average > 50% of CPU count\n  DISK_THRESHOLD: 0.95, // One-at-a-time if disk usage > 95%\n\n  // API limit thresholds (usage ratios: 0.0 - 1.0)\n  CLAUDE_SESSION_THRESHOLD: 0.9, // Stop if 5-hour limit > 90%\n  CLAUDE_WEEKLY_THRESHOLD: 0.99, // One-at-a-time if weekly limit > 99%\n  GITHUB_API_THRESHOLD: 0.8, // Stop if GitHub > 80% with parallel claude\n\n  // Timing\n  // MIN_START_INTERVAL_MS: Time to allow solve command to start actual claude process\n  // This ensures that when API limits are checked, the running process is counted\n  MIN_START_INTERVAL_MS: 120000, // 2 minutes between starts (was 1 minute)\n  CONSUMER_POLL_INTERVAL_MS: 60000, // 1 minute between queue checks (was 5 seconds)\n  MESSAGE_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS: 60000, // 1 minute between status message updates\n\n  // Process detection\n  CLAUDE_PROCESS_NAMES: ['claude'], // Process names to detect\n};\n\n/**\n * Status enum for queue items\n */\nexport const QueueItemStatus = {\n  QUEUED: 'queued',\n  WAITING: 'waiting',\n  STARTING: 'starting',\n  STARTED: 'started',\n  FAILED: 'failed',\n  CANCELLED: 'cancelled',\n};\n\n/**\n * Count running claude processes\n * @param {boolean} verbose - Whether to log verbose output\n * @returns {Promise<{count: number, processes: string[]}>}\n */\nexport async function getRunningClaudeProcesses(verbose = false) {\n  try {\n    const { stdout } = await execAsync('pgrep -l -x claude 2>/dev/null || true');\n    const lines = stdout\n      .trim()\n      .split('\\n')\n      .filter(line => line.trim());\n\n    const processes = lines\n      .map(line => {\n        const parts = line.trim().split(/\\s+/);\n        return {\n          pid: parts[0],\n          name: parts.slice(1).join(' ') || 'claude',\n        };\n      })\n      .filter(p => p.pid);\n\n    if (verbose) {\n      console.log(`[VERBOSE] /solve-queue found ${processes.length} running claude processes`);\n      if (processes.length > 0) {\n        console.log(`[VERBOSE] /solve-queue processes: ${JSON.stringify(processes)}`);\n      }\n    }\n\n    return {\n      count: processes.length,\n      processes: processes.map(p => `${p.pid}:${p.name}`),\n    };\n  } catch (error) {\n    if (verbose) {\n      console.error('[VERBOSE] /solve-queue error counting claude processes:', error.message);\n    }\n    return { count: 0, processes: [] };\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Format a threshold as percentage for display\n * @param {number} ratio - Ratio (0.0 - 1.0)\n * @returns {string} Formatted percentage\n */\nfunction formatThresholdPercent(ratio) {\n  return `${Math.round(ratio * 100)}%`;\n}\n\n/**\n * Generate human-readable waiting reason based on threshold violation\n * @param {string} metric - The metric name (ram, cpu, disk, etc.)\n * @param {number} currentValue - Current value (as percentage 0-100)\n * @param {number} threshold - Threshold ratio (0.0 - 1.0)\n * @returns {string} Human-readable reason\n */\nfunction formatWaitingReason(metric, currentValue, threshold) {\n  const thresholdPercent = formatThresholdPercent(threshold);\n  const currentPercent = Math.round(currentValue);\n\n  switch (metric) {\n    case 'ram':\n      return `RAM usage is ${currentPercent}% (threshold: ${thresholdPercent})`;\n    case 'cpu':\n      return `CPU usage is ${currentPercent}% (threshold: ${thresholdPercent})`;\n    case 'disk':\n      return `Disk usage is ${currentPercent}% (threshold: ${thresholdPercent})`;\n    case 'claude_session':\n      return `Claude session limit is ${currentPercent}% (threshold: ${thresholdPercent})`;\n    case 'claude_weekly':\n      return `Claude weekly limit is ${currentPercent}% (threshold: ${thresholdPercent})`;\n    case 'github':\n      return `GitHub API usage is ${currentPercent}% (threshold: ${thresholdPercent})`;\n    case 'min_interval':\n      return `Minimum interval between commands not reached`;\n    case 'claude_running':\n      return `Claude process is already running`;\n    default:\n      return `${metric} threshold exceeded`;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Queue item representing a /solve command request\n */\nclass SolveQueueItem {\n  constructor(options) {\n    this.id = `solve-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).substring(2, 9)}`;\n    this.url = options.url;\n    this.args = options.args;\n    this.ctx = options.ctx;\n    this.requester = options.requester;\n    this.infoBlock = options.infoBlock;\n    this.tool = options.tool || 'claude';\n    this.createdAt = new Date();\n    this.startedAt = null;\n    this.status = QueueItemStatus.QUEUED;\n    this.waitingReason = null;\n    this.error = null;\n    this.result = null;\n    this.sessionName = null;\n    // Message tracking - forget after STARTED\n    this.messageIn
[2026-01-09T23:54:51.941Z] [INFO] fo = null; // { chatId, messageId }\n    // Track when we last updated the Telegram message\n    // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1078\n    this.lastMessageUpdateTime = null;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Update status to waiting with reason\n   * @param {string} reason - Waiting reason\n   */\n  setWaiting(reason) {\n    this.status = QueueItemStatus.WAITING;\n    this.waitingReason = reason;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Update status to starting\n   */\n  setStarting() {\n    this.status = QueueItemStatus.STARTING;\n    this.startedAt = new Date();\n    this.waitingReason = null;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Update status to started and clear message tracking\n   * @param {string} sessionName - Session name for debugging\n   */\n  setStarted(sessionName) {\n    this.status = QueueItemStatus.STARTED;\n    this.sessionName = sessionName;\n    // Terminal status - forget message tracking\n    this.messageInfo = null;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Mark item as failed\n   * @param {Error|string} error - Error that occurred\n   */\n  setFailed(error) {\n    this.status = QueueItemStatus.FAILED;\n    this.error = error instanceof Error ? error.message : error;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Mark item as cancelled\n   */\n  setCancelled() {\n    this.status = QueueItemStatus.CANCELLED;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get wait time in queue (ms)\n   */\n  getWaitTime() {\n    const endTime = this.startedAt || new Date();\n    return endTime - this.createdAt;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Format for display\n   * @returns {string}\n   */\n  toString() {\n    return `[${this.id}] ${this.url} (${this.status})`;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Solve Queue - Producer/Consumer queue for /solve commands\n */\nexport class SolveQueue {\n  constructor(options = {}) {\n    this.verbose = options.verbose || false;\n    this.executeCallback = options.executeCallback || null;\n    this.messageUpdateCallback = options.messageUpdateCallback || null;\n\n    // Queue state\n    this.queue = [];\n    this.processing = new Map();\n    this.completed = [];\n    this.failed = [];\n    this.isRunning = true;\n\n    // Timing\n    this.lastStartTime = null;\n\n    // Consumer task reference\n    this.consumerTask = null;\n\n    // Statistics\n    this.stats = {\n      totalEnqueued: 0,\n      totalStarted: 0,\n      totalCompleted: 0,\n      totalFailed: 0,\n      totalCancelled: 0,\n      throttleReasons: {},\n    };\n\n    this.log('SolveQueue initialized');\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Log message if verbose mode is enabled\n   * @param {string} message\n   */\n  log(message) {\n    if (this.verbose) {\n      console.log(`[VERBOSE] /solve-queue: ${message}`);\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Add a solve command to the queue\n   * @param {Object} options - Queue item options\n   * @returns {SolveQueueItem} The queued item\n   */\n  enqueue(options) {\n    const item = new SolveQueueItem(options);\n    this.queue.push(item);\n    this.stats.totalEnqueued++;\n\n    this.log(`Enqueued: ${item.toString()}, queue length: ${this.queue.length}`);\n\n    // Start consumer if not already running\n    this.ensureConsumerRunning();\n\n    return item;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Cancel a queued item by ID\n   * @param {string} id - Item ID\n   * @returns {boolean} True if cancelled\n   */\n  cancel(id) {\n    const queueIndex = this.queue.findIndex(item => item.id === id);\n    if (queueIndex !== -1) {\n      const item = this.queue.splice(queueIndex, 1)[0];\n      item.setCancelled();\n      this.stats.totalCancelled++;\n      this.log(`Cancelled queued item: ${item.toString()}`);\n      return true;\n    }\n\n    if (this.processing.has(id)) {\n      this.log(`Cannot cancel processing item: ${id}`);\n      return false;\n    }\n\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get queue statistics\n   * @returns {Object}\n   */\n  getStats() {\n    return {\n      queued: this.queue.length,\n      processing: this.processing.size,\n      completed: this.completed.length,\n      failed: this.failed.length,\n      ...this.stats,\n      cacheStats: getLimitCache().getStats(),\n      lastStartTime: this.lastStartTime,\n      isRunning: this.isRunning,\n    };\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get queue items summary for display\n   * @returns {Object}\n   */\n  getQueueSummary() {\n    return {\n      pending: this.queue.map(item => ({\n        id: item.id,\n        url: item.url,\n        requester: item.requester,\n        waitTime: item.getWaitTime(),\n        createdAt: item.createdAt,\n        status: item.status,\n        waitingReason: item.waitingReason,\n      })),\n      processing: Array.from(this.processing.values()).map(item => ({\n        id: item.id,\n        url: item.url,\n        requester: item.requester,\n        startedAt: item.startedAt,\n        status: item.status,\n      })),\n    };\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Check if a new command can start\n   *\n   * Logic per issue #1061:\n   * 1. \"Claude process is already running\" is NOT a limit by itself - it's a metric\n   * 2. Commands can run in parallel as long as actual limits are not exceeded\n   * 3. When any limit >= threshold, allow exactly one claude command to pass\n   *\n   * @returns {Promise<{canStart: boolean, reason?: string, reasons?: string[], oneAtATime?: boolean}>}\n   */\n  async canStartCommand() {\n    const reasons = [];\n    let oneAtATime = false;\n\n    // Check minimum interval since last start\n    if (this.lastStartTime) {\n      const timeSinceLastStart = Date.now() - this.lastStartTime;\n      if (timeSinceLastStart < QUEUE_CONFIG.MIN_START_INTERVAL_MS) {\n        const waitSeconds = Math.ceil((QUEUE_CONFIG.MIN_START_INTERVAL_MS - timeSinceLastStart) / 1000);\n        reasons.push(formatWaitingReason('min_interval', 0, 0) + ` (${waitSeconds}s remaining)`);\n        this.recordThrottle('min_interval');\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Check running claude processes (this is a metric, not a blocking reason by itself)\n    const claudeProcs = await getRunningClaudeProcesses(this.verbose);\n    const hasRunningClaude = claudeProcs.count > 0;\n\n    // Track claude_running as a metric (but don't add to reasons yet)\n    if (hasRunningClaude) {\n      this.recordThrottle('claude_running');\n    }\n\n    // Check system resources\n    const resourceCheck = await this.checkSystemResources();\n    if (!resourceCheck.ok) {\n      reasons.push(...resourceCheck.reasons);\n    }\n    if (resourceCheck.oneAtATime) {\n      oneAtATime = true;\n    }\n\n    // Check API limits (pass hasRunningClaude to enable special handling)\n    const limitCheck = await this.checkApiLimits(hasRunningClaude);\n    if (!limitCheck.ok) {\n      reasons.push(...limitCheck.reasons);\n    }\n    if (limitCheck.oneAtATime) {\n      oneAtATime = true;\n    }\n\n    // \"Claude process running\" only blocks if there are OTHER reasons too\n    // This allows parallel execution when limits are not exceeded\n    if (hasRunningClaude && reasons.length > 0) {\n      // Add claude_running info at the END (not beginning) of reasons\n      // Since it's supplementary info, not the primary blocking reason\n      // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1078\n      reasons.push(formatWaitingReason('claude_running', claudeProcs.count, 0) + ` (${claudeProcs.count} processes)`);\n    }\n\n    const canStart = reasons.length === 0;\n\n    if (!canStart && this.verbose) {\n      this.log(`Cannot start: ${reasons.join(', ')}`);\n    }\n\n    return {\n      canStart,\n      reason: reasons.length > 0 ? reasons.join('\\n') : undefined,\n      reasons,\n      oneAtATime,\n      claudeProcesses: claudeProcs.count,\n    };\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Check system resources (RAM, CPU, disk) using cached values\n   *\n   * Uses 5-minute load average for CPU instead of instantaneous usage.\n   * This provides a more stable metric that isn't affected by brief spikes\n   * during claude process startup.\n   *\n   * @returns {Promise<{ok: boolean, reasons: string[], oneAtATime: boolean}>}\n   */\n  async checkSystemResources() {\n    const reasons = [];\n    let oneAtATime = false;\n\n    // Check RAM (using cached value)\n    const memResult = await getCachedMemoryInfo(this.verbose);\n    if (memResult.success) {\n      const usedRatio = memResult.memory.usedPercentage / 100;\n      if (usedRatio > QUEUE_CONFIG.RAM_THRESHOLD) {\n        reasons.push(formatWaitingReason('ram', memResult.memory.usedPercentage, QUEUE_CONFIG.RAM_THRESHOLD));\n        this.recordThrottle('ram_high');\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Check CPU using 5-minute load average (more stable than 1-minute)\n    // Cache TTL is 2 minutes, which is appropriate for this metric\n    const cpuResult = await getCachedCpuInfo(this.verbose);\n    if (cpuResult.success) {\n      // Use loadAvg5 (5-minute average) instead of usagePercentage (1-minute based)\n      // This provides a more stable metric that isn't affected by transient spikes\n      const loadAvg5 = cpuResult.cpuLoad.loadAvg5;\n      const cpuCount = cpuResult.cpuLoad.cpuCount;\n      // Calculate usage ratio: loadAvg5 / cpuCount\n      // Load average of 1.0 per CPU = 100% utilization\n      const usageRatio = loadAvg5 / cpuCount;\n      const usagePercent = Math.min(100, Math.round(usageRatio * 100));\n\n      if (this.verbose) {\n        this.log(`CPU 5m load avg: ${loadAvg5.toFixed(2)}, cpus: ${cpuCount}, usage: ${usagePercent}%`);\n      }\n\n      if (usageRatio > QUEUE_CONFIG.CPU_THRESHOLD) {\n        reasons.push(formatWaitingReason('cpu', usagePercent, QUEUE_CONFIG.CPU_THRESHOLD));\n        this.recordThrottle('cpu_high');\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Check disk space (using cached value)\n    const diskResult = await getCachedDiskInfo(this.verbose);\n    if (diskResult.success) {\n      // Calculate usage from free percentage\n      const usedPercent = 100 - diskResult.diskSpace.freePercentage;\n      const usedRatio = usedPercent / 100;\n      if (usedRatio > QUEUE_CONFIG.DISK_THRESHOLD) {\n        oneAtATime = true;\n        this.recordThrottle('disk_high');\n        if (this.processing.size > 0) {\n          reasons.push(formatWaitingReason('disk', usedPercent, QUEUE_CONFIG.DISK_THRESHOLD) + ' (waiting for current command)');\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    return { ok: reasons.length === 0, reasons, oneAtATime };\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Check API limits (Claude, GitHub) using cached values\n   *\n   * Simplified logic per issue #1061:\n   * - When any limit >= threshold, allow exactly one claude command to pass\n   * - Only block if there's already a command in progress\n   * - Running claude is the ultimate test of whether limits are really exhausted\n   *\n   * @param {boolean} hasRunningClaude - Whether claude processes are running\n   * @returns {Promise<{ok: boolean, reasons: string[], oneAtATime: boolean}>}\n   */\n  async checkApiLimits(hasRunningClaude = false) {\n    const reasons = [];\n    let oneAtATime = false;\n\n    // Check Claude limits (using cached value)\n    const claudeResult = await getCachedClaudeLimits(this.verbose);\n    if (claudeResult.success) {\n      const sessionPercent = claudeResult.usage.currentSession.percentage;\n      const weeklyPercent = claudeResult.usage.allModels.percentage;\n\n      // Session limit (5-hour)\n      // When above threshold: allow exactly one command, block if one is running\n      if (sessionPercent !== null) {\n        const sessionRatio = sessionPercent / 100;\n        if (sessionRatio >= QUEUE_CONFIG.CLAUDE_SESSION_THRESHOLD) {\n          // Only block if Claude is already running\n          if (hasRunningClaude) {\n            reasons.push(formatWaitingReason('claude_session', sessionPercent, QUEUE_CONFIG.CLAUDE_SESSION_THRESHOLD));\n          }\n          this.recordThrottle(sessionRatio >= 1.0 ? 'claude_session_100' : 'claude_session_high');\n        }\n      }\n\n      // Weekly limit\n      // When above threshold: allow exactly one command, block if one is in progress\n      if (weeklyPercent !== null) {\n        const weeklyRatio = weeklyPercent / 100;\n        if (weeklyRatio >= QUEUE_CONFIG.CLAUDE_WEEKLY_THRESHOLD) {\n          oneAtATime = true;\n          this.recordThrottle(weeklyRatio >= 1.0 ? 'claude_weekly_100' : 'claude_weekly_high');\n          // Only block if command is already in progress\n          if (this.processing.size > 0) {\n            reasons.push(formatWaitingReason('claude_weekly', weeklyPercent, QUEUE_CONFIG.CLAUDE_WEEKLY_THRESHOLD) + ' (waiting for current command)');\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Check GitHub limits (only relevant if claude processes running)\n    if (hasRunningClaude) {\n      const githubResult = await getCachedGitHubLimits(this.verbose);\n      if (githubResult.success) {\n        const usedPercent = githubResult.githubRateLimit.usedPercentage;\n        const usedRatio = usedPercent / 100;\n        if (usedRatio >= QUEUE_CONFIG.GITHUB_API_THRESHOLD) {\n          reasons.push(formatWaitingReason('github', usedPercent, QUEUE_CONFIG.GITHUB_API_THRESHOLD));\n          this.recordThrottle(usedRatio >= 1.0 ? 'github_100' : 'github_high');\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    return { ok: reasons.length === 0, reasons, oneAtATime };\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Record a throttle event for statistics\n   * @param {string} reason\n   */\n  recordThrottle(reason) {\n    this.stats.throttleReasons[reason] = (this.stats.throttleReasons[reason] || 0) + 1;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Ensure consumer task is running\n   */\n  ensureConsumerRunning() {\n    if (this.consumerTask) return;\n\n    this.consumerTask = this.runConsumer();\n    this.consumerTask.catch(error => {\n      console.error('[solve-queue] Consumer error:', error);\n      this.consumerTask = null;\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Update item message in Telegram\n   * @param {SolveQueueItem} item\n   * @param {string} text\n   * @param {boolean} trackUpdateTime - Whether to track this as a periodic update (default: true)\n   */\n  async updateItemMessage(item, text, trackUpdateTime = true) {\n    if (!item.messageInfo || !item.ctx) return;\n\n    try {\n      const { chatId, messageId } = item.messageInfo;\n      await item.ctx.telegram.editMessageText(chatId, messageId, undefined, text, { parse_mode: 'Markdown' });\n      if (trackUpdateTime) {\n        item.lastMessageUpdateTime = Date.now();\n      }\n    } catch (error) {\n      this.log(`Failed to update message: ${error.message}`);\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Check if an item's message should be updated periodically\n   * @param {SolveQueueItem} item\n   * @returns {boolean}\n   */\n  shouldUpdateMessage(item) {\n    if (!item.messageInfo || !item.ctx) return false;\n    if (!item.lastMessageUpdateTime) return true; // Never updated\n    return Date.now() - item.lastMessageUpdateTime >= QUEUE_CONFIG.MESSAGE_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Consumer loop - processes items from the queue\n   */\n  async runConsumer() {\n    this.log('Consumer started');\n\n    while (this.isRunning) {\n      if (this.queue.length === 0) {\n        await this.sleep(QUEUE_CONFIG.CONSUMER_POLL_INTERVAL_MS);\n        continue;\n      }\n\n      const check = await this.canStartCommand();\n\n      if (!check.canStart) {\n        // Update all queued items to waiting status with reason\n        // Also periodically refresh messages to show current status\n        // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1078\n        for (const item of this.queue) {\n          if (item.status === QueueItemStatus.QUEUED || item.status === QueueItemStatus.WAITING) {\n            const previousStatus = item.status;\n            const previousReason = item.waitingReason;\n            item.setWaiting(check.reason);\n\n            // Update message if:\n            // 1. Status or reason changed\n            // 2. OR it's time for a periodic update (every MESSAGE_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS)\n            const shouldUpdate = previousStatus !== item.status || previousReason !== item.waitingReason || this.shouldUpdateMessage(item);\n\n            if (shouldUpdate) {\n              const position = this.queue.indexOf(item) + 1;\n              await this.updateItemMessage(item, `⏳ Waiting (position #${position})\\n\\n${item.infoBlock}\\n\\n*Reason:*\\n${check.reason}`);\n            }\n          }\n        }\n\n        this.log(`Throttled: ${check.reason}`);\n        await this.sleep(QUEUE_CONFIG.CONSUMER_POLL_INTERVAL_MS);\n        continue;\n      }\n\n      // Check one-at-a-time mode\n      if (check.oneAtATime && this.processing.size > 0) {\n        this.log('One-at-a-time mode: waiting for current command to finish');\n        await this.sleep(QUEUE_CONFIG.CONSUMER_POLL_INTERVAL_MS);\n        continue;\n      }\n\n      // Get next item from queue\n      const item = this.queue.shift();\n      if (!item) continue;\n\n      // NOTE: Running claude processes is NOT a blocking limit by itself\n      // Commands can run in parallel as long as actual limits (CPU, API, etc.) are not exceeded\n      // The MIN_START_INTERVAL_MS ensures enough time for processes to be counted\n      // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1078\n\n      // Update status to Starting\n      item.setStarting();\n      this.processing.set(item.id, item);\n      this.lastStartTime = Date.now();\n      this.stats.totalStarted++;\n\n      // Update message to show Starting status\n      await this.updateItemMessage(item, `🚀 Starting solve command...\\n\\n${item.infoBlock}`);\n\n      this.log(`Starting: ${item.toString()}`);\n\n      // Execute in background\n      this.executeItem(item).catch(error => {\n        console.error(`[solve-queue] Execution error for ${item.id}:`, error);\n      });\n    }\n\n    this.log('Consumer stopped');\n    this.consumerTask = null;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Execute a queue item\n   * @param {SolveQueueItem} item\n   */\n  async executeItem(item) {\n    try {\n      if (this.executeCallback) {\n        const result = await this.executeCallback(item);\n\n        // Extract session name from result\n        let sessionName = 'unknown';\n        if (result && result.output) {\n          const sessionMatch = result.output.match(/session:\\s*(\\S+)/i) || result.output.match(/screen -r\\s+(\\S+)/);\n          if (sessionMatch) sessionName = sessionMatch[1];\n        }\n\n        // IMPORTANT: Save messageInfo BEFORE calling setStarted, because setStarted clears it\n        // This was a bug where the final message update never happened because messageInfo was null\n        // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1062\n        const savedMessageInfo = item.messageInfo;\n\n        // Update to Started status (terminal - forgets message tracking)\n        item.setStarted(sessionName);\n        this.stats.totalCompleted++;\n\n        // Final message update using saved messageInfo\n        if (item.ctx && result && savedMessageInfo) {\n          const { chatId, messageId } = savedMessageInfo;\n          if (chatId && messageId) {\n            try {\n              if (result.warning) {\n                await item.ctx.telegram.editMessageText(chatId, messageId, undefined, `⚠️ ${result.warning}`, { parse_mode: 'Markdown' });\n              } else if (result.success) {\n                const response = `✅ Solve command started successfully!\\n\\n📊 Session: \\`${sessionName}\\`\\n\\n${item.infoBlock}`;\n                await item.ctx.telegram.editMessageText(chatId, messageId, undefined, response, { parse_mode: 'Markdown' });\n              } else {\n                const response = `❌ Error executing solve command:\\n\\n\\`\\`\\`\\n${result.error || result.output}\\n\\`\\`\\``;\n                await item.ctx.telegram.editMessageText(chatId, messageId, undefined, response, { parse_mode: 'Markdown' });\n              }\n            } catch (error) {\n              // Log message edit failures for debugging\n              // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1062\n              console.error(`[solve-queue] Failed to update message for item ${item.id}: ${error.message}`);\n            }\n          }\n        }\n      } else {\n        item.setStarted('no-callback');\n        this.stats.totalCompleted++;\n      }\n    } catch (error) {\n      item.setFailed(error);\n      this.stats.totalFailed++;\n      console.error(`[solve-queue] Item failed: ${item.id}`, error);\n\n      // Try to update message with error\n      const { chatId, messageId } = item.messageInfo || {};\n      if (chatId && messageId && item.ctx) {\n        try {\n          await item.ctx.telegram.editMessageText(chatId, messageId, undefined, `❌ Error: ${error.message}`, { parse_mode: 'Markdown' });\n        } catch (editError) {\n          // Log the edit failure for debugging\n          // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1062\n          console.error(`[solve-queue] Failed to update error message for item ${item.id}: ${editError.message}`);\n        }\n      }\n    } finally {\n      this.processing.delete(item.id);\n\n      if (item.status === QueueItemStatus.STARTED) {\n        this.completed.push(item);\n      } else if (item.status === QueueItemStatus.FAILED) {\n        this.failed.push(item);\n      }\n\n      this.log(`Finished: ${item.toString()}`);\n\n      // Limit history size\n      while (this.completed.length > 100) this.completed.shift();\n      while (this.failed.length > 100) this.failed.shift();\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Sleep for specified milliseconds\n   * @param {number} ms\n   * @returns {Promise<void>}\n   */\n  sleep(ms) {\n    return new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, ms));\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Stop the queue\n   */\n  stop() {\n    this.log('Stopping queue...');\n    this.isRunning = false;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Clear the limit cache\n   */\n  clearCache() {\n    getLimitCache().clear();\n    this.log('Limit cache cleared');\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Format queue status for display\n   * @returns {string}\n   */\n  formatStatus() {\n    const stats = this.getStats();\n    if (stats.queued > 0 || stats.processing > 0) {\n      return `Solve Queue: ${stats.queued} pending, ${stats.processing} processing\\n`;\n    }\n    return 'Solve Queue: empty\\n';\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Format detailed queue status for Telegram message\n   * @returns {string}\n   */\n  formatDetailedStatus() {\n    const stats = this.getStats();\n    const summary = this.getQueueSummary();\n\n    let message = '📋 *Solve Queue Status*\\n\\n';\n    message += `Pending: ${stats.queued}\\n`;\n    message += `Processing: ${stats.processing}\\n`;\n    message += `Completed: ${stats.completed}\\n`;\n    message += `Failed: ${stats.failed}\\n\\n`;\n\n    if (summary.processing.length > 0) {\n      message += '*Currently Processing:*\\n';\n      for (const item of summary.processing) {\n        message += `• ${item.url}\\n`;\n      }\n      message += '\\n';\n    }\n\n    if (summary.pending.length > 0) {\n      message += '*Waiting in Queue:*\\n';\n      for (const item of summary.pending.slice(0, 5)) {\n        const waitSeconds = Math.floor(item.waitTime / 1000);\n        message += `• ${item.url} (${item.status}, ${waitSeconds}s)\\n`;\n        if (item.waitingReason) {\n          message += `  └ ${item.waitingReason}\\n`;\n        }\n      }\n      if (summary.pending.length > 5) {\n        message += `  ... and ${summary.pending.length - 5} more\\n`;\n      }\n    }\n\n    return message;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Global queue instance (singleton)\n */\nlet globalQueue = null;\n\n/**\n * Get or create the global solve queue instance\n * @param {Object} options - Queue options\n * @returns {SolveQueue}\n */\nexport function getSolveQueue(options = {}) {\n  if (!globalQueue) {\n    globalQueue = new SolveQueue(options);\n  } else if (options.verbose !== undefined) {\n    globalQueue.verbose = options.verbose;\n  }\n  return globalQueue;\n}\n\n/**\n * Reset the global queue (useful for testing)\n */\nexport function resetSolveQueue() {\n  if (globalQueue) {\n    globalQueue.stop();\n    globalQueue = null;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Create an execute callback for the queue\n * @param {Function} executeStartScreen - Function to execute start-screen command\n * @returns {Function} Execute callback for queue items\n */\nexport function createQueueExecuteCallback(executeStartScreen) {\n  return async item => {\n    return await executeStartScreen('solve', item.args);\n  };\n}\n\nexport default {\n  SolveQueue,\n  SolveQueueItem,\n  getSolveQueue,\n  resetSolveQueue,\n  getRunningClaudeProcesses,\n  createQueueExecuteCallback,\n  QUEUE_CONFIG,\n  QueueItemStatus,\n};\n","numLines":913,"startLine":1,"totalLines":913}}}

function executeStartScreen(command, args) {\n   406→  try {\n   407→    // Check if start-screen is available BEFORE first execution\n   408→    const whichPath = await findStartScreenCommand();\n   409→\n   410→    if (!whichPath) {\n   411→      const warningMsg = '⚠️  WARNING: start-screen command not found in PATH\\n' + 'Please ensure @link-assistant/hive-mind is properly installed\\n' + 'You may need to run: npm install -g @link-assistant/hive-mind';\n   412→      console.warn(warningMsg);\n   413→\n   414→      // Still try to execute with 'start-screen' in case it's available in PATH
