420→    }\n   421→\n   422→    // Use the resolved path from which\n   423→    if (VERBOSE) {\n   424→      console.log(`[VERBOSE] Found start-screen at: ${whichPath}`);\n   425→    }\n   426→\n   427→    return await executeWithCommand(whichPath, command, args);\n   428→  } catch (error) {\n   429→    console.error('Error executing start-screen:', error);\n   430→    return {\n   431→      success: false,\n   432→      output: '',\n   433→      error: error.message,\n   434→    };\n   435→  }\n   436→}\n   437→\n   438→function executeWithCommand(startScreenCmd, command, args) {\n   439→  return new Promise(resolve => {\n   440→    const allArgs = [command, ...args];\n   441→\n   442→    if (VERBOSE) {\n   443→      console.log(`[VERBOSE] Executing: ${startScreenCmd} ${allArgs.join(' ')}`);\n   444→    } else {\n   445→      console.log(`Executing: ${startScreenCmd} ${allArgs.join(' ')}`);\n   446→    }\n   447→\n   448→    const child = spawn(startScreenCmd, allArgs, {\n   449→      stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe'],\n   450→      detached: false,\n   451→      env: process.env,\n   452→    });\n   453→\n   454→    let stdout = '';\n   455→    let stderr = '';\n   456→\n   457→    child.stdout.on('data', data => {\n   458→      stdout += data.toString();\n   459→    });\n   460→\n   461→    child.stderr.on('data', data => {\n   462→      stderr += data.toString();\n   463→    });\n   464→\n   465→    child.on('error', error => {\n   466→      resolve({\n   467→        success: false,\n   468→        output: stdout,\n   469→        error: error.message,\n   470→      });\n   471→    });\n   472→\n   473→    child.on('close', code => {\n   474→      if (code === 0) {\n   475→        resolve({\n   476→          success: true,\n   477→          output: stdout,\n   478→        });\n   479→      } else {\n   480→        resolve({\n   481→          success: false,\n   482→          output: stdout,\n   483→          error: stderr || `Command exited with code ${code}`,\n   484→        });\n   485→      }\n   486→    });\n   487→  });\n   488→}\n   489→\n   490→/**\n   491→ * Validates the model name in the args array and returns an error message if invalid\n   492→ * @param {string[]} args - Array of command arguments\n   493→ * @param {string} tool - The tool to validate against ('claude' or 'opencode')\n   494→ * @returns {string|null} Error message if invalid, null if valid or no model specified\n   495→ */\n   496→function validateModelInArgs(args, tool = 'claude') {\n   497→  // Find --model or -m flag and its value\n   498→  for (let i = 0; i < args.length; i++) {\n   499→    if (args[i] === '--model' || args[i] === '-m') {\n   500→      if (i + 1 < args.length) {\n   501→        const modelName = args[i + 1];\n   502→        const validation = validateModelName(modelName, tool);\n   503→        if (!validation.valid) {\n   504→          return validation.message;\n   505→        }\n   506→      }\n   507→    } else if (args[i].startsWith('--model=')) {\n   508→      const modelName = args[i].substring('--model='.length);\n   509→      const validation = validateModelName(modelName, tool);\n   510→      if (!validation.valid) {\n   511→        return validation.message;\n   512→      }\n   513→    }\n   514→  }\n   515→  return null;\n   516→}\n   517→\n   518→function parseCommandArgs(text) {\n   519→  // Use only first line and trim it\n   520→  const firstLine = text.split('\\n')[0].trim();\n   521→  const argsText = firstLine.replace(/^\\/\\w+\\s*/, '');\n   522→\n   523→  if (!argsText.trim()) {\n   524→    return [];\n   525→  }\n   526→\n   527→  // Replace em-dash (—) with double-dash (--) to fix Telegram auto-replacement\n   528→  const normalizedArgsText = argsText.replace(/—/g, '--');\n   529→\n   530→  const args = [];\n   531→  let currentArg = '';\n   532→  let inQuotes = false;\n   533→  let quoteChar = null;\n   534→\n   535→  for (let i = 0; i < normalizedArgsText.length; i++) {\n   536→    const char = normalizedArgsText[i];\n   537→\n   538→    if ((char === '\"' || char === \"'\") && !inQuotes) {\n   539→      inQuotes = true;\n   540→      quoteChar = char;\n   541→    } else if (char === quoteChar && inQuotes) {\n   542→      inQuotes = false;\n   543→      quoteChar = null;\n   544→    } else if (char === ' ' && !inQuotes) {\n   545→      if (currentArg) {\n   546→        args.push(currentArg);\n   547→        currentArg = '';\n   548→      }\n   549→    } else {\n   550→      currentArg += char;\n   551→    }\n   552→  }\n   553→\n   554→  if (currentArg) {\n   555→    args.push(currentArg);\n   556→  }\n   557→\n   558→  return args;\n   559→}\n   560→\n   561→function mergeArgsWithOverrides(userArgs, overrides) {\n   562→  if (!overrides || overrides.length === 0) {\n   563→    return userArgs;\n   564→  }\n   565→\n   566→  // Parse overrides to identify flags and their values\n   567→  const overrideFlags = new Map(); // Map of flag -> value (or null for boolean flags)\n   568→\n   569→  for (let i = 0; i < overrides.length; i++) {\n   570→    const arg = overrides[i];\n   571→    if (arg.startsWith('--')) {\n   572→      // Check if next item is a value (doesn't start with --)\n   573→      if (i + 1 < overrides.length && !overrides[i + 1].startsWith('--')) {\n   574→        overrideFlags.set(arg, overrides[i + 1]);\n   575→        i++; // Skip the value in next iteration\n   576→      } else {\n   577→        overrideFlags.set(arg, null); // Boolean flag\n   578→      }\n   579→    }\n   580→  }\n   581→\n   582→  // Filter user args to remove any that conflict with overrides\n   583→  const filteredArgs = [];\n   584→  for (let i = 0; i < userArgs.length; i++) {\n   585→    const arg = userArgs[i];\n   586→    if (arg.startsWith('--')) {\n   587→      // If this flag exists in overrides, skip it and its value\n   588→      if (overrideFlags.has(arg)) {\n   589→        // Skip the flag\n   590→        // Also skip next arg if it's a value (doesn't start with --)\n   591→        if (i + 1 < userArgs.length && !userArgs[i + 1].startsWith('--')) {\n   592→          i++; // Skip the value too\n   593→        }\n   594→        continue;\n   595→      }\n   596→    }\n   597→    filteredArgs.push(arg);\n   598→  }\n   599→\n   600→  // Merge: filtered user args + overrides\n   601→  return [...filteredArgs, ...overrides];\n   602→}\n   603→\n   604→/**\n   605→ * Validate GitHub URL for Telegram bot commands\n   606→ *\n   607→ * @param {string[]} args - Command arguments (first arg should be URL)\n   608→ * @param {Object} options - Validation options\n   609→ * @param {string[]} options.allowedTypes - Allowed URL types (e.g., ['issue', 'pull'] or ['repository', 'organization', 'user'])\n   610→ * @param {string} options.commandName - Command name for error messages (e.g., 'solve' or 'hive')\n   611→ * @param {string} options.exampleUrl - Example URL for error messages\n   612→ * @returns {{ valid: boolean, error?: string }}\n   613→ */\n   614→function validateGitHubUrl(args, options = {}) {\n   615→  // Default options for /solve command (backward compatibility)\n   616→  const { allowedTypes = ['issue', 'pull'], commandName = 'solve' } = options;\n   617→\n   618→  if (args.length === 0) {\n   619→    return {\n   620→      valid: false,\n   621→      error: `Missing GitHub URL. Usage: /${commandName} <github-url> [options]`,\n   622→    };\n   623→  }\n   624→\n   625→  const url = args[0];\n   626→  if (!url.includes('github.com')) {\n   627→    return {\n   628→      valid: false,\n   629→      error: 'First argument must be a GitHub URL',\n   630→    };\n   631→  }\n   632→\n   633→  // Parse the URL to validate structure\n   634→  const parsed = parseGitHubUrl(url);\n   635→  if (!parsed.valid) {\n   636→    return {\n   637→      valid: false,\n   638→      error: parsed.error || 'Invalid GitHub URL',\n   639→      suggestion: parsed.suggestion,\n   640→    };\n   641→  }\n   642→\n   643→  // Check if the URL type is allowed for this command\n   644→  if (!allowedTypes.includes(parsed.type)) {\n   645→    const allowedTypesStr = allowedTypes.map(t => (t === 'pull' ? 'pull request' : t)).join(', ');\n   646→    const baseUrl = `https://github.com/${parsed.owner}/${parsed.repo}`;\n   647→\n   648→    // Provide specific, helpful error messages based on the URL type\n   649→    let error;\n   650→    if (parsed.type === 'issues_list') {\n   651→      error = `URL points to the issues list page, but you need a specific issue\\n\\n💡 How to fix:\\n1. Open the repository: ${url}\\n2. Click on a specific issue\\n3. Copy the URL (it should end with /issues/NUMBER)\\n\\nExample: \\`${baseUrl}/issues/1\\``;\n   652→    } else if (parsed.type === 'pulls_list') {\n   653→      error = `URL points to the pull requests list page, but you need a specific pull request\\n\\n💡 How to fix:\\n1. Open the repository: ${url}\\n2. Click on a specific pull request\\n3. Copy the URL (it should end with /pull/NUMBER)\\n\\nExample: \\`${baseUrl}/pull/1\\``;\n   654→    } else if (parsed.type === 'repo') {\n   655→      error = `URL points to a repository, but you need a specific ${allowedTypesStr}\\n\\n💡 How to fix:\\n1. Go to: ${url}/issues\\n2. Click on an issue to solve\\n3. Use the full URL with the issue number\\n\\nExample: \\`${baseUrl}/issues/1\\``;\n   656→    } else {\n   657→      error = `URL must be a GitHub ${allowedTypesStr} (not ${parsed.type.replace('_', ' ')})`;\n   658→    }\n   659→\n   660→    return { valid: false, error };\n   661→  }\n   662→\n   663→  return { valid: true };\n   664→}\n   665→\n   666→/**\n   667→ * Escape special characters for Telegram's legacy Markdown parser.\n   668→ * In Telegram's Markdown, these characters need escaping: _ * [ ] ( ) ~ ` > # + - = | { } . !\n   669→ * However, for plain text (not inside markup), we primarily need to escape _ and *\n   670→ * to prevent them from being interpreted as formatting.\n   671→ *\n   672→ * @param {string} text - Text to escape\n   673→ * @returns {string} Escaped text safe for Markdown parse_mode\n   674→ */\n   675→/**\n   676→ * Execute a start-screen command and update the initial message with the result.\n   677→ * Used by both /solve and /hive commands to reduce code duplication.\n   678→ *\n   679→ * @param {Object} ctx - Telegram context\n   680→ * @param {Object} startingMessage - The initial message to update\n   681→ * @param {string} commandName - Command name (e.g., 'solve' or 'hive')\n   682→ * @param {string[]} args - Command arguments\n   683→ * @param {string} infoBlock - Info block with request details\n   684→ */\n   685→async function executeAndUpdateMessage(ctx, startingMessage, commandName, args, infoBlock) {\n   686→  const result = await executeStartScreen(commandName, args);\n   687→  const { chat, message_id } = startingMessage;\n   688→\n   689→  // Safely edit message - catch errors to prevent stuck \"Starting...\" messages (issue #1062)\n   690→  const safeEdit = async text => {\n   691→    try {\n   692→      await ctx.telegram.editMessageText(chat.id, message_id, undefined, text, { parse_mode: 'Markdown' });\n   693→    } catch (e) {\n   694→      console.error(`[telegram-bot] Failed to update message for ${commandName}: ${e.message}`);\n   695→    }\n   696→  };\n   697→\n   698→  if (result.warning) return safeEdit(`⚠️  ${result.warning}`);\n   699→\n   700→  if (result.success) {\n   701→    const match = result.output.match(/session:\\s*(\\S+)/i) || result.output.match(/screen -r\\s+(\\S+)/);\n   702→    const session = match ? match[1] : 'unknown';\n   703→    await safeEdit(`✅ ${commandName.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + commandName.slice(1)} command started successfully!\\n\\n📊 Session: \\`${session}\\`\\n\\n${infoBlock}`);\n   704→  } else {\n   705→    await safeEdit(`❌ Error executing ${commandName} command:\\n\\n\\`\\`\\`\\n${result.error || result.output}\\n\\`\\`\\``);\n   706→  }\n   707→}\n   708→\n   709→/**\n   710→ * Extract GitHub issue/PR URL from message text\n   711→ * Validates that message contains exactly one GitHub issue/PR link\n   712→ *\n   713→ * @param {string} text - Message text to search\n   714→ * @returns {{ url: string|null, error: string|null, linkCount: number }}\n   715→ */\n   716→function extractGitHubUrl(text) {\n   717→  if (!text || typeof text !== 'string') {\n   718→    return { url: null, error: null, linkCount: 0 };\n   719→  }\n   720→\n   721→  text = cleanNonPrintableChars(text); // Clean non-printable chars before processing\n   722→  const words = text.split(/\\s+/);\n   723→  const foundUrls = [];\n   724→\n   725→  for (const word of words) {\n   726→    // Try to parse as GitHub URL\n   727→    const parsed = parseGitHubUrl(word);\n   728→\n   729→    // Accept issue or PR URLs\n   730→    if (parsed.valid && (parsed.type === 'issue' || parsed.type === 'pull')) {\n   731→      foundUrls.push(parsed.normalized);\n   732→    }\n   733→  }\n   734→\n   735→  // Check if multiple links were found\n   736→  if (foundUrls.length === 0) {\n   737→    return { url: null, error: null, linkCount: 0 };\n   738→  } else if (foundUrls.length === 1) {\n   739→    return { url: foundUrls[0], error: null, linkCount: 1 };\n   740→  } else {\n   741→    return {\n   742→      url: null,\n   743→      error: `Found ${foundUrls.length} GitHub links in the message. Please reply to a message with only one GitHub issue or PR link.`,\n   744→      linkCount: foundUrls.length,\n   745→    };\n   746→  }\n   747→}\n   748→\n   749→bot.command('help', async ctx => {\n   750→  if (VERBOSE) {\n   751→    console.log('[VERBOSE] /help command received');\n   752→  }\n   753→\n   754→  // Ignore messages sent before bot started\n   755→  if (isOldMessage(ctx)) {\n   756→    if (VERBOSE) {\n   757→      console.log('[VERBOSE] /help ignored: old message');\n   758→    }\n   759→    return;\n   760→  }\n   761→\n   762→  // Ignore forwarded or reply messages\n   763→  if (isForwardedOrReply(ctx)) {\n   764→    if (VERBOSE) {\n   765→      console.log('[VERBOSE] /help ignored: forwarded or reply');\n   766→    }\n   767→    return;\n   768→  }\n   769→\n   770→  const chatId = ctx.chat.id;\n   771→  const chatType = ctx.chat.type;\n   772→  const chatTitle = ctx.chat.title || 'Private Chat';\n   773→\n   774→  let message = '🤖 *SwarmMindBot Help*\\n\\n';\n   775→  message += '📋 *Diagnostic Information:*\\n';\n   776→  message += `• Chat ID: \\`${chatId}\\`\\n`;\n   777→  message += `• Chat Type: ${chatType}\\n`;\n   778→  message += `• Chat Title: ${chatTitle}\\n\\n`;\n   779→  message += '📝 *Available Commands:*\\n\\n';\n   780→\n   781→  if (solveEnabled) {\n   782→    message += '*/solve* - Solve a GitHub issue\\n';\n   783→    message += 'Usage: `/solve <github-url> [options]`\\n';\n   784→    message += 'Example: `/solve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123 --model sonnet`\\n';\n   785→    message += 'Or reply to a message with a GitHub link: `/solve`\\n';\n   786→    if (solveOverrides.length > 0) {\n   787→      message += `🔒 Locked options: \\`${solveOverrides.join(' ')}\\`\\n`;\n   788→    }\n   789→    message += '\\n';\n   790→  } else {\n   791→    message += '*/solve* - ❌ Disabled\\n\\n';\n   792→  }\n   793→\n   794→  if (hiveEnabled) {\n   795→    message += '*/hive* - Run hive command\\n';\n   796→    message += 'Usage: `/hive <github-url> [options]`\\n';\n   797→    message += 'Example: `/hive https://github.com/owner/repo`\\n';\n   798→    if (hiveOverrides.length > 0) {\n   799→      message += `🔒 Locked options: \\`${hiveOverrides.join(' ')}\\`\\n`;\n   800→    }\n   801→    message += '\\n';\n   802→  } else {\n   803→    message += '*/hive* - ❌ Disabled\\n\\n';\n   804→  }\n   805→\n   806→  message += '*/limits* - Show usage limits\\n';\n   807→  message += '*/version* - Show bot and runtime versions\\n';\n   808→  message += '*/help* - Show this help message\\n\\n';\n   809→  message += '⚠️ *Note:* /solve, /hive, /limits and /version commands only work in group chats.\\n\\n';\n   810→  message += '🔧 *Common Options:*\\n';\n   811→  message += '• `--model <model>` or `-m` - Specify AI model (sonnet, opus, haiku, haiku-3-5, haiku-3)\\n';\n   812→  message += '• `--base-branch <branch>` or `-b` - Target branch for PR (default: repo default branch)\\n';\n   813→  message += '• `--think <level>` - Thinking level (low/medium/high/max)\\n';\n   814→  message += '• `--verbose` or `-v` - Verbose output | `--attach-logs` - Attach logs to PR\\n';\n   815→  message += '\\n💡 *Tip:* Many more options available. See full documentation for complete list.\\n';\n   816→\n   817→  if (allowedChats) {\n   818→    message += '\\n🔒 *Restricted Mode:* This bot only accepts commands from authorized chats.\\n';\n   819→    message += `Authorized: ${isChatAuthorized(chatId) ? '✅ Yes' : '❌ No'}`;\n   820→  }\n   821→\n   822→  message += '\\n\\n🔧 *Troubleshooting:*\\n';\n   823→  message += 'If bot is not receiving messages:\\n';\n   824→  message += '1. Check privacy mode in @BotFather\\n';\n   825→  message += '   • Send `/setprivacy` to @BotFather\\n';\n   826→  message += '   • Choose \"Disable\" for your bot\\n';\n   827→  message += '   • Remove bot from group and re-add\\n';\n   828→  message += '2. Or make bot an admin in the group\\n';\n   829→  message += '3. Restart bot with `--verbose` flag for diagnostics';\n   830→\n   831→  await ctx.reply(message, { parse_mode: 'Markdown' });\n   832→});\n   833→\n   834→bot.command('limits', async ctx => {\n   835→  if (VERBOSE) {\n   836→    console.log('[VERBOSE] /limits command received');\n   837→  }\n   838→\n   839→  // Add breadcrumb for error tracking\n   840→  await addBreadcrumb({\n   841→    category: 'telegram.command',\n   842→    message: '/limits command received',\n   843→    level: 'info',\n   844→    data: {\n   845→      chatId: ctx.chat?.id,\n   846→      chatType: ctx.chat?.type,\n   847→      userId: ctx.from?.id,\n   848→      username: ctx.from?.username,\n   849→    },\n   850→  });\n   851→\n   852→  // Ignore messages sent before bot started\n   853→  if (isOldMessage(ctx)) {\n   854→    if (VERBOSE) {\n   855→      console.log('[VERBOSE] /limits ignored: old message');\n   856→    }\n   857→    return;\n   858→  }\n   859→\n   860→  // Ignore forwarded or reply messages\n   861→  if (isForwardedOrReply(ctx)) {\n   862→    if (VERBOSE) {\n   863→      console.log('[VERBOSE] /limits ignored: forwarded or reply');\n   864→    }\n   865→    return;\n   866→  }\n   867→\n   868→  if (!isGroupChat(ctx)) {\n   869→    if (VERBOSE) {\n   870→      console.log('[VERBOSE] /limits ignored: not a group chat');\n   871→    }\n   872→    await ctx.reply('❌ The /limits command only works in group chats. Please add this bot to a group and make it an admin.', { reply_to_message_id: ctx.message.message_id });\n   873→    return;\n   874→  }\n   875→\n   876→  const chatId = ctx.chat.id;\n   877→  if (!isChatAuthorized(chatId)) {\n   878→    if (VERBOSE) {\n   879→      console.log('[VERBOSE] /limits ignored: chat not authorized');\n   880→    }\n   881→    await ctx.reply(`❌ This chat (ID: ${chatId}) is not authorized to use this bot. Please contact the bot administrator.`, { reply_to_message_id: ctx.message.message_id });\n   882→    return;\n   883→  }\n   884→\n   885→  // Send \"fetching\" message to indicate work is in progress\n   886→  const fetchingMessage = await ctx.reply('🔄 Fetching usage limits...', {\n   887→    reply_to_message_id: ctx.message.message_id,\n   888→  });\n   889→\n   890→  // Get all limits using shared cache (3min for API, 2min for system)\n   891→  const limits = await getAllCachedLimits(VERBOSE);\n   892→\n   893→  if (!limits.claude.success) {\n   894→    const escapedError = escapeMarkdownV2(limits.claude.error, { preserveCodeBlocks: true });\n   895→    await ctx.telegram.editMessageText(fetchingMessage.chat.id, fetchingMessage.message_id, undefined, `❌ ${escapedError}`, { parse_mode: 'MarkdownV2' });\n   896→    return;\n   897→  }\n   898→\n   899→  // Format the message with usage limits and queue status\n   900→  let message = '📊 *Usage Limits*\\n\\n' + formatUsageMessage(limits.claude.usage, limits.disk.success ? limits.disk.diskSpace : null, limits.github.success ? limits.github.githubRateLimit : null, limits.cpu.success ? limits.cpu.cpuLoad : null, limits.memory.success ? limits.memory.memory : null);\n   901→  const solveQueue = getSolveQueue({ verbose: VERBOSE });\n   902→  const queueStats = solveQueue.getStats();\n   903→  const claudeProcs = await getRunningClaudeProcesses(VERBOSE);\n   904→  const codeBlockEnd = message.lastIndexOf('```');\n   905→  if (codeBlockEnd !== -1) {\n   906→    const queueStatus = queueStats.queued > 0 || queueStats.processing > 0 ? `Pending: ${queueStats.queued}, Processing: ${queueStats.processing}` : 'Empty (no pending commands)';\n   907→    message = message.slice(0, codeBlockEnd) + `\\nSolve Queue\\n${queueStatus}\\nClaude processes: ${claudeProcs.count}\\n` + message.slice(codeBlockEnd);\n   908→  }\n   909→  await ctx.telegram.editMessageText(fetchingMessage.chat.id, fetchingMessage.message_id, undefined, message, { parse_mode: 'Markdown' });\n   910→});\n   911→bot.command('version', async ctx => {\n   912→  VERBOSE && console.log('[VERBOSE] /version command received');\n   913→  await addBreadcrumb({\n   914→    category: 'telegram.command',\n   915→    message: '/version command received',\n   916→    level: 'info',\n   917→    data: { chatId: ctx.chat?.id, chatType: ctx.chat?.type, userId: ctx.from?.id, username: ctx.from?.username },\n   918→  });\n   919→  if (isOldMessage(ctx) || isForwardedOrReply(ctx)) return;\n   920→  if (!isGroupChat(ctx)) return await ctx.reply('❌ The /version command only works in group chats. Please add this bot to a group and make it an admin.', { reply_to_message_id: ctx.message.message_id });\n   921→  const chatId = ctx.chat.id;\n   922→  if (!isChatAuthorized(chatId)) return await ctx.reply(`❌ This chat (ID: ${chatId}) is not authorized to use this bot. Please contact the bot administrator.`, { reply_to_message_id: ctx.message.message_id });\n   923→  const fetchingMessage = await ctx.reply('🔄 Gathering version information...', {\n   924→    reply_to_message_id: ctx.message.message_id,\n   925→  });\n   926→  const result = await getVersionInfo(VERBOSE);\n   927→  if (!result.success) return await ctx.telegram.editMessageText(fetchingMessage.chat.id, fetchingMessage.message_id, undefined, `❌ ${escapeMarkdownV2(result.error, { preserveCodeBlocks: true })}`, { parse_mode: 'MarkdownV2' });\n   928→  await ctx.telegram.editMessageText(fetchingMessage.chat.id, fetchingMessage.message_id, undefined, '🤖 *Version Information*\\n\\n' + formatVersionMessage(result.versions), { parse_mode: 'Markdown' });\n   929→});\n   930→bot.command(/^solve$/i, async ctx => {\n   931→  if (VERBOSE) {\n   932→    console.log('[VERBOSE] /solve command received');\n   933→  }\n   934→\n   935→  // Add breadcrumb for error tracking\n   936→  await addBreadcrumb({\n   937→    category: 'telegram.command',\n   938→    message: '/solve command received',\n   939→    level: 'info',\n   940→    data: {\n   941→      chatId: ctx.chat?.id,\n   942→      chatType: ctx.chat?.type,\n   943→      userId: ctx.from?.id,\n   944→      username: ctx.from?.username,\n   945→    },\n   946→  });\n   947→\n   948→  if (!solveEnabled) {\n   949→    if (VERBOSE) {\n   950→      console.log('[VERBOSE] /solve ignored: command disabled');\n   951→    }\n   952→    await ctx.reply('❌ The /solve command is disabled on this bot instance.');\n   953→    return;\n   954→  }\n   955→\n   956→  // Ignore messages sent before bot started\n   957→  if (isOldMessage(ctx)) {\n   958→    if (VERBOSE) {\n   959→ .replace(/token[s]?\\s*[:=]\\s*[\\w-]+/gi, 'token: [REDACTED]')\n  1354→            .replace(/password[s]?\\s*[:=]\\s*[\\w-]+/gi, 'password: [REDACTED]')\n  1355→ Executing: ${startScreenCmd} ${allArgs.join(' ')}`);\n    } else {\n      console.log(`Executing: ${startScreenCmd} ${allArgs.join(' ')}`);\n    }\n\n    const child = spawn(startScreenCmd, allArgs,       output: stdout,\n        error: error.message,\n      });\n    });\n\n    child.on('close', code => {\n      if (code === 0) {\n        resolve({\n          success: true,\n          output: });\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Validates the model name in the args array and returns an error message if invalid\n * @param {string[]} args - Array of command arguments\n * @param {string} tool - The tool to validate against ('claude' or 'opencode')\n * @returns {string|null} Error message if invalid, null if valid or no model specified\n */\nfunction validateModelInArgs(args, tool = 'claude') {\n  // Find --model or -m flag and its value\n  for (let i = 0; i < args.length; i++) {\n    if (args[i] === '--model' || args[i] === '-m') {\n      if (i + 1 < args.length) {\n        const modelName = args[i + 1];\n        const validation = validateModelName(modelName, tool);\n        if (!validation.valid) {\n          return validation.message;\n        }\n      }\n    } else if (args[i].startsWith('--model=')) {\n      const modelName = args[i].substring('--model='.length);\n      const validation = validateModelName(modelName, tool);\n      if (!validation.valid) {\n        return validation.message;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  return null;\n}\n\nfunction parseCommandArgs(text) {\n  // Use only first line and trim it\n  const firstLine = text.split('\\n')[0].trim();\n  const argsText = firstLine.replace(/^\\/\\w+\\s*/, '');\n\n  if (!argsText.trim()) {\n    return [];\n  }\n\n  // Replace em-dash (—) with double-dash (--) to fix Telegram auto-replacement\n  const normalizedArgsText = argsText.replace(/—/g, '--');\n\n  const args = [];\n  let currentArg = '';\n  let inQuotes = false;\n  let quoteChar = null;\n\n  for (let i = 0; i < normalizedArgsText.length; i++) {\n    const char = normalizedArgsText[i];\n\n    if ((char === '\"' || char === \"'\") && !inQuotes) {\n      inQuotes = true;\n      quoteChar = char;\n    } else if (char === quoteChar && inQuotes) {\n      inQuotes = false;\n      quoteChar = null;\n    } else if (char === ' ' && !inQuotes) {\n      if (currentArg) {\n        args.push(currentArg);\n        currentArg = '';\n      }\n    } else {\n      currentArg += char;\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (currentArg) {\n    args.push(currentArg);\n  }\n\n  return args;\n}\n\nfunction mergeArgsWithOverrides(userArgs, overrides) {\n  if (!overrides || overrides.length === 0) {\n    return userArgs;\n  }\n\n  // Parse overrides to identify flags and their values\n  const overrideFlags = new Map(); // Map of flag -> value (or null for boolean flags)\n\n  for (let i = 0; i < overrides.length; i++) {\n    const arg = overrides[i];\n    if (arg.startsWith('--')) {\n      // Check if next item is a value (doesn't start with --)\n      if (i + 1 < overrides.length && !overrides[i + 1].startsWith('--')) {\n        overrideFlags.set(arg, overrides[i + 1]);\n        i++; // Skip the value in next iteration\n      } else {\n        overrideFlags.set(arg, null); // Boolean flag\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Filter user args to remove any that conflict with overrides\n  const filteredArgs = [];\n  for (let i = 0; i < userArgs.length; i++) {\n    const arg = userArgs[i];\n    if (arg.startsWith('--')) {\n      // If this flag exists in overrides, skip it and its value\n      if (overrideFlags.has(arg)) {\n        // Skip the flag\n        // Also skip next arg if it's a value (doesn't start with --)\n        if (i + 1 < userArgs.length && !userArgs[i + 1].startsWith('--')) {\n          i++; // Skip the value too\n        }\n        continue;\n      }\n    }\n    filteredArgs.push(arg);\n  }\n\n  // Merge: filtered user args + overrides\n  return [...filteredArgs, ...overrides];\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate GitHub URL for Telegram bot commands\n *\n * @param {string[]} args - Command arguments (first arg should be URL)\n * @param {Object} options - Validation options\n * @param {string[]} options.allowedTypes - Allowed URL types (e.g., ['issue', 'pull'] or ['repository', 'organization', 'user'])\n * @param {string} options.commandName - Command name for error messages (e.g., 'solve' or 'hive')\n * @param {string} options.exampleUrl - Example URL for error messages\n * @returns {{ valid: boolean, error?: string }}\n */\nfunction validateGitHubUrl(args, options = {}) {\n  // Default options for /solve command (backward compatibility)\n  const { allowedTypes = ['issue', 'pull'], commandName = 'solve' } = options;\n\n  if (args.length === 0) {\n    return {\n      valid: false,\n      error: `Missing GitHub URL. Usage: /${commandName} <github-url> [options]`,\n    };\n  }\n\n  const url = args[0];\n  if (!url.includes('github.com')) {\n    return {\n      valid: false,\n      error: 'First argument must be a GitHub URL',\n    };\n  }\n\n  // Parse the URL to validate structure\n  const parsed = parseGitHubUrl(url);\n  if (!parsed.valid) {\n    return {\n      valid: false,\n      error: parsed.error || 'Invalid GitHub URL',\n      suggestion: parsed.suggestion,\n    };\n  }\n\n  // Check if the URL type is allowed for this command\n  if (!allowedTypes.includes(parsed.type)) {\n    const allowedTypesStr = allowedTypes.map(t => (t === 'pull' ? 'pull request' : t)).join(', ');\n    const baseUrl = `https://github.com/${parsed.owner}/${parsed.repo}`;\n\n    // Provide specific, helpful error messages based on the URL type\n    let error;\n    if (parsed.type === 'issues_list') {\n      error = `URL points to the issues list page, but you need a specific issue\\n\\n💡 How to fix:\\n1. Open the repository: ${url}\\n2. Click on a specific issue\\n3. Copy the URL (it should end with /issues/NUMBER)\\n\\nExample: \\`${baseUrl}/issues/1\\``;\n    } else if (parsed.type === 'pulls_list') {\n      error = `URL points to the pull requests list page, but you need a specific pull request\\n\\n💡 How to fix:\\n1. Open the repository: ${url}\\n2. Click on a specific pull request\\n3. Copy the URL (it should end with /pull/NUMBER)\\n\\nExample: \\`${baseUrl}/pull/1\\``;\n    } else if (parsed.type === 'repo') {\n      error = `URL points to a repository, but you need a specific ${allowedTypesStr}\\n\\n💡 How to fix:\\n1. Go to: ${url}/issues\\n2. Click on an issue to solve\\n3. Use the full URL with the issue number\\n\\nExample: \\`${baseUrl}/issues/1\\``;\n    } else {\n      error = `URL must be a GitHub ${allowedTypesStr} (not ${parsed.type.replace('_', ' ')})`;\n    }\n\n    return { valid: false, error };\n  }\n\n  return { valid: true };\n}\n\n/**\n * Escape special characters for Telegram's legacy Markdown parser.\n * In Telegram's Markdown, these characters need escaping: _ * [ ] ( ) ~ ` > # + - = | { } . !\n * However, for plain text (not inside markup), we primarily need to escape _ and *\n * to prevent them from being interpreted as formatting.\n *\n * @param {string} text - Text to escape\n * @returns {string} Escaped text safe for Markdown parse_mode\n */\n/**\n * Execute a start-screen command and update the initial message with the result.\n * Used by both /solve and /hive commands to reduce code duplication.\n *\n * @param {Object} ctx - Telegram context\n * @param {Object} startingMessage - The initial message to update\n * @param {string} commandName - Command name (e.g., 'solve' or 'hive')\n * @param {string[]} args - Command arguments\n * @param {string} infoBlock - Info block with request details\n */\nasync function executeAndUpdateMessage(ctx, startingMessage, commandName, args, infoBlock) {\n  const result = await executeStartScreen(commandName, args);\n  const { chat, message_id } = startingMessage;\n\n  // Safely edit message - catch errors to prevent stuck \"Starting...\" messages (issue #1062)\n  const safeEdit = async text => {\n    try {\n      await ctx.telegram.editMessageText(chat.id, message_id, undefined, text, { parse_mode: 'Markdown' });\n    } catch (e) {\n      console.error(`[telegram-bot] Failed to update message for ${commandName}: ${e.message}`);\n    }\n  };\n\n  if (result.warning) return safeEdit(`⚠️  ${result.warning}`);\n\n  if (result.success) {\n    const match = result.output.match(/session:\\s*(\\S+)/i) || result.output.match(/screen -r\\s+(\\S+)/);\n    const session = match ? match[1] : 'unknown';\n    await safeEdit(`✅ ${commandName.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + commandName.slice(1)} command started successfully!\\n\\n📊 Session: \\`${session}\\`\\n\\n${infoBlock}`);\n  } else {\n    await safeEdit(`❌ Error executing ${commandName} command:\\n\\n\\`\\`\\`\\n${result.error || result.output}\\n\\`\\`\\``);\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Extract GitHub issue/PR URL from message text\n * Validates that message contains exactly one GitHub issue/PR link\n *\n * @param {string} text - Message text to search\n * @returns {{ url: string|null, error: string|null, linkCount: number }}\n */\nfunction extractGitHubUrl(text) {\n  if (!text || typeof text !== 'string') {\n    return { url: null, error: null, linkCount: 0 };\n  }\n\n  text = cleanNonPrintableChars(text); // Clean non-printable chars before processing\n  const words = text.split(/\\s+/);\n  const foundUrls = [];\n\n  for (const word of words) {\n    // Try to parse as GitHub URL\n    const parsed = parseGitHubUrl(word);\n\n    // Accept issue or PR URLs\n    if (parsed.valid && (parsed.type === 'issue' || parsed.type === 'pull')) {\n      foundUrls.push(parsed.normalized);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check if multiple links were found\n  if (foundUrls.length === 0) {\n    return { url: null, error: null, linkCount: 0 };\n  } else if (foundUrls.length === 1) {\n    return { url: foundUrls[0], error: null, linkCount: 1 };\n  } else {\n    return {\n      url: null,\n      error: `Found ${foundUrls.length} GitHub links in the message. Please reply to a message with only one GitHub issue or PR link.`,\n      linkCount: foundUrls.length,\n    };\n  }\n}\n\nbot.command('help', async ctx => {\n  if (VERBOSE) {\n    console.log('[VERBOSE] /help command received');\n  }\n\n  // Ignore messages sent before bot started\n  if (isOldMessage(ctx)) {\n    if (VERBOSE) {\n      console.log('[VERBOSE] /help ignored: old message');\n    }\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Ignore forwarded or reply messages\n  if (isForwardedOrReply(ctx)) {\n    if (VERBOSE) {\n      console.log('[VERBOSE] /help ignored: forwarded or reply');\n    }\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const chatId = ctx.chat.id;\n  const chatType = ctx.chat.type;\n  const chatTitle = ctx.chat.title || 'Private Chat';\n\n  let message = '🤖 *SwarmMindBot Help*\\n\\n';\n  message += '📋 *Diagnostic Information:*\\n';\n  message += `• Chat ID: \\`${chatId}\\`\\n`;\n  message += `• Chat Type: ${chatType}\\n`;\n  message += `• Chat Title: ${chatTitle}\\n\\n`;\n  message += '📝 *Available Commands:*\\n\\n';\n\n  if (solveEnabled) {\n    message += '*/solve* - Solve a GitHub issue\\n';\n    message += 'Usage: `/solve <github-url> [options]`\\n';\n    message += 'Example: `/solve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123 --model sonnet`\\n';\n    message += 'Or reply to a message with a GitHub link: `/solve`\\n';\n    if (solveOverrides.length > 0) {\n      message += `🔒 Locked options: \\`${solveOverrides.join(' ')}\\`\\n`;\n    }\n    message += '\\n';\n  } else {\n    message += '*/solve* - ❌ Disabled\\n\\n';\n  }\n\n  message += '*/limits* - Show usage limits\\n';\n  message += '*/version* - Show bot and runtime versions\\n';\n  message += '*/help* - Show this help message\\n\\n';\n  message += '⚠️ *Note:* /solve, /hive, /limits and /version commands only work in group chats.\\n\\n';\n  message += '🔧 *Common Options:*\\n';\n  message += '• `--model <model>` or `-m` - Specify AI model (sonnet, opus, haiku, haiku-3-5, haiku-3)\\n';\n  message += '• `--base-branch <branch>` or `-b` - Target branch for PR (default: repo default branch)\\n';\n  message += '• `--think <level>` - Thinking level (low/medium/high/max)\\n';\n  message += '• `--verbose` or `-v` - Verbose output | `--attach-logs` - Attach logs to PR\\n';\n  message += '\\n💡 *Tip:* Many more options available. See full documentation for complete list.\\n';\n\n  if (allowedChats) {\n    message += '\\n🔒 *Restricted Mode:* This bot only accepts commands from authorized chats.\\n';\n    message += `Authorized: ${isChatAuthorized(chatId) ? '✅ Yes' : '❌ No'}`;\n  }\n\n  message += '\\n\\n🔧 *Troubleshooting:*\\n';\n  message += 'If bot is not receiving messages:\\n';\n  message += '1. Check privacy mode in @BotFather\\n';\n  message += '   • Send `/setprivacy` to @BotFather\\n';\n  message += '   • Choose \"Disable\" for your bot\\n';\n  message += '   • Remove bot from group and re-add\\n';\n  message += '2. Or make bot an admin in the group\\n';\n  message += '3. Restart bot with `--verbose` flag for diagnostics';\n\n  await ctx.reply(message, { parse_mode: 'Markdown' });\n});\n\nbot.command('limits', async ctx => {\n  if (VERBOSE) {\n    console.log('[VERBOSE] /limits command received');\n  }\n\n  // Add breadcrumb for error tracking\n  await addBreadcrumb({\n    category: 'telegram.command',\n    message: '/limits command received',\n    level: 'info',\n    data: {\n      chatId: ctx.chat?.id,\n      chatType: ctx.chat?.type,\n      userId: ctx.from?.id,\n      username: ctx.from?.username,\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Ignore messages sent before bot started\n  if (isOldMessage(ctx)) {\n    if (VERBOSE) {\n      console.log('[VERBOSE] /limits ignored: old message');\n    }\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Ignore forwarded or reply messages\n  if (isForwardedOrReply(ctx)) {\n    if (VERBOSE) {\n      console.log('[VERBOSE] /limits ignored: forwarded or reply');\n    }\n    return;\n  }\n\n  if (!isGroupChat(ctx)) {\n    if (VERBOSE) {\n      console.log('[VERBOSE] /limits ignored: not a group chat');\n    }\n    await ctx.reply('❌ The /limits command only works in group chats. Please add this bot to a group and make it an admin.', { reply_to_message_id: ctx.message.message_id });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const chatId = ctx.chat.id;\n  if (!isChatAuthorized(chatId)) {\n    if (VERBOSE) {\n      console.log('[VERBOSE] /limits ignored: chat not authorized');\n    }\n    await ctx.reply(`❌ This chat (ID: ${chatId}) is not authorized to use this bot. Please contact the bot administrator.`, { reply_to_message_id: ctx.message.message_id });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Send \"fetching\" message to indicate work is in progress\n  const fetchingMessage = await ctx.reply('🔄 Fetching usage limits...', {\n    reply_to_message_id: ctx.message.message_id,\n  });\n\n  // Get all limits using shared cache (3min for API, 2min for system)\n  const limits = await getAllCachedLimits(VERBOSE);\n\n  if (!limits.claude.success) {\n    const escapedError = escapeMarkdownV2(limits.claude.error, { preserveCodeBlocks: true });\n    await ctx.telegram.editMessageText(fetchingMessage.chat.id, fetchingMessage.message_id, undefined, `❌ ${escapedError}`, { parse_mode: 'MarkdownV2' });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Format the message with usage limits and queue status\n  let message = '📊 *Usage Limits*\\n\\n' + formatUsageMessage(limits.claude.usage, limits.disk.success ? limits.disk.diskSpace : null, limits.github.success ? limits.github.githubRateLimit : null, limits.cpu.success ? limits.cpu.cpuLoad : null, limits.memory.success ? limits.memory.memory : null);\n  const solveQueue = getSolveQueue({ verbose: VERBOSE });\n  const queueStats = solveQueue.getStats();\n  const claudeProcs = await getRunningClaudeProcesses(VERBOSE);\n  const codeBlockEnd = message.lastIndexOf('```');\n  if (codeBlockEnd !== -1) {\n    const queueStatus = queueStats.queued > 0 || queueStats.processing > 0 ? `Pending: ${queueStats.queued}, Processing: ${queueStats.processing}` : 'Empty (no pending commands)';\n    message = message.slice(0, codeBlockEnd) + `\\nSolve Queue\\n${queueStatus}\\nClaude processes: ${claudeProcs.count}\\n` + message.slice(codeBlockEnd);\n  }\n  await ctx.telegram.editMessageText(fetchingMessage.chat.id, fetchingMessage.message_id, undefined, message, { parse_mode: 'Markdown' });\n});\nbot.command('version', async ctx => {\n  VERBOSE && console.log('[VERBOSE] /version command received');\n  await addBreadcrumb({\n    category: 'telegram.command',\n    message: '/version command received',\n    level: 'info',\n    data: { chatId: ctx.chat?.id, chatType: ctx.chat?.type, userId: ctx.from?.id, username: ctx.from?.username },\n  });\n  if (isOldMessage(ctx) || isForwardedOrReply(ctx)) return;\n  if (!isGroupChat(ctx)) return await ctx.reply('❌ The /version command only works in group chats. Please add this bot to a group and make it an admin.', { reply_to_message_id: ctx.message.message_id });\n  const chatId = ctx.chat.id;\n  if (!isChatAuthorized(chatId)) return await ctx.reply(`❌ This chat (ID: ${chatId}) is not authorized to use this bot. fetchingMessage.message_id, undefined, '🤖 *Version Information*\\n\\n' + formatVersionMessage(result.versions), { parse_mode: 'Markdown' });\n});\nbot.command(/^solve$/i, async ctx => {\n  if (VERBOSE) {\n    console.log('[VERBOSE] /solve command received');\n  }\n\n  // Add breadcrumb for error tracking\n  await addBreadcrumb({\n    category: 'telegram.command',\n    message: '/solve command received',\n    level: 'info',\n    data: {\n      chatId: ctx.chat?.id,\n      chatType: ctx.chat?.type,\n      userId: ctx.from?.id,\n      username: ctx.from?.username,\n    },\n  });\n\n  if (!solveEnabled) {\n    if (VERBOSE) {\n      console.log('[VERBOSE] /solve ignored: command disabled');\n    }\n    await ctx.reply('❌ The /solve command is disabled on this bot instance.');\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Ignore messages sent before bot started\n  if (isOldMessage(ctx)) {\n    if (VERBOSE) {\n      console.log('[VERBOSE] /solve ignored: old message');\n    }\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Check if this is a forwarded message (not allowed)\n  // But allow reply messages for URL extraction feature\n  const message = ctx.message;\n  const isForwarded = message.forward_origin && message.forward_sender_name || message.forward_date;\n\n  if (isForwarded || isOldApiForwarded) {\n    if (VERBOSE) {\n      console.log('[VERBOSE] /solve ignored: forwarded message');\n    }\n    return;\n  }\n\n  if (!isGroupChat(ctx)) {\n    if (VERBOSE) {\n      console.log('[VERBOSE] /solve ignored: not a group chat');\n    }\n    await ctx.reply('❌ The /solve command only works in group }\n\n  let userArgs = parseCommandArgs(ctx.message.text);\n\n  // Check if this is a reply to a message and user didn't provide URL\n  // In that case, try to extract GitHub URL from the replied message\n  const isReply = message.reply_to_message && message.reply_to_message.message_id && !message.reply_to_message.forum_topic_created;\n\n  if (isReply && userArgs.length === 0) {\n    if (VERBOSE) {\n      console.log('[VERBOSE] /solve is a reply without URL, extracting from replied message...');\n    }\n\n    const replyText = message.reply_to_message.text || '';\n    const extraction = extractGitHubUrl(replyText);\n\n    if (extraction.error) {\n      // Multiple links found\n      if (VERBOSE) {\n        console.log('[VERBOSE] Multiple GitHub URLs found in replied message');\n      }\n      await ctx.reply(`❌ ${extraction.error}`, {\n        parse_mode: 'Markdown',\n        reply_to_message_id: ctx.message.message_id,\n      });\n      return;\n    } else if (extraction.url) {\n      // Single link found\n      if (VERBOSE) {\n        console.log('[VERBOSE] Extracted URL from reply:', extraction.url);\n      }\n      // Add the extracted URL as the first argument\n      userArgs = [extraction.url];\n    } else {\n      // No link found\n      if (VERBOSE) {\n        console.log('[VERBOSE] No GitHub URL found in replied message');\n      }\n      await ctx.reply('❌ No GitHub issue/PR link found in the replied message.\\n\\nExample: Reply to a message containing a GitHub issue link with `/solve`', { parse_mode: 'Markdown', reply_to_message_id: ctx.message.message_id });\n      return;\n    }\n  }\n\n  const validation = validateGitHubUrl(userArgs);\n  if (!validation.valid) {\n    let errorMsg = `❌ ${validation.error}`;\n    if (validation.suggestion) {\n      errorMsg += `\\n\\n💡 Did you mean: \\`${validation.suggestion}\\``;\n    }\n    errorMsg += '\\n\\nExample: `/solve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123`\\n\\nOr reply to a message containing a GitHub link with `/solve`';\n    await ctx.reply(errorMsg, { parse_mode: 'Markdown', reply_to_message_id: ctx.message.message_id });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Merge user args with overrides\n  const args = mergeArgsWithOverrides(userArgs, solveOverrides);\n\n  // Determine tool from args (default: claude)\n  let solveTool = 'claude';\n  for (let i = 0; i < args.length; i++) {\n    if (args[i] === '--tool' && i + 1 < args.length) {\n      solveTool = args[i + 1];\n    } else if (args[i].startsWith('--tool=')) {\n      solveTool = args[i].substring('--tool='.length);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Validate model name with helpful error message (before yargs validation)\n  const modelError = validateModelInArgs(args, solveTool);\n  if (modelError) {\n    await ctx.reply(`❌ ${modelError}`, { parse_mode: 'Markdown', reply_to_message_id: ctx.message.message_id });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Validate merged arguments using solve's yargs config\n  try {\n    // Use .parse() 
[2026-01-09T23:54:51.977Z] [INFO] instead of yargs(args).parseSync() to ensure .strict() mode works\n    const testYargs = createSolveYargsConfig(yargs());\n\n    // Configure yargs to throw errors instead of trying to exit the process\n    // This prevents confusing error messages when validation fails but execution continues\n    let failureMessage = null;\n    testYargs.exitProcess(false).fail((msg, err) => {\n      // Capture the failure message instead of letting yargs print it\n      failureMessage = msg || (err && err.message) || 'Unknown validation error';\n      throw new Error(failureMessage);\n    });\n\n    testYargs.parse(args);\n  } catch (error) {\n    await ctx.reply(`❌ Invalid options: ${error.message || String(error)}\\n\\nUse /help to see available options`, {\n      parse_mode: 'Markdown',\n      reply_to_message_id: ctx.message.message_id,\n    });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const requester = buildUserMention({ user: ctx.from, parseMode: 'Markdown' });\n  const optionsText = args.slice(1).join(' ') || 'none';\n  let infoBlock = `Requested by: ${requester}\\nURL: ${escapeMarkdown(args[0])}\\nOptions: ${optionsText}`;\n  if (solveOverrides.length > 0) infoBlock += `\\n🔒 Locked options: ${solveOverrides.join(' ')}`;\n  const solveQueue = getSolveQueue({ verbose: VERBOSE });\n  const check = await This is safe to add to the end of your bot's entry file.\n\nThe recommended pattern is:\n\n```javascript\nprocess.once('SIGINT', () => bot.stop('SIGINT'));\nprocess.once('SIGTERM', () => bot.stop('SIGTERM'));\n```\n\n### Complete Example\n\nThe official documentation shows the pattern with `bot.launch()` followed by the graceful stop handlers for both SIGINT and SIGTERM signals.\n\n```javascript\nconst { Telegraf } = require('telegraf')\nconst { message } = require('telegraf/filters')\n\nconst bot = new Telegraf(process.env.BOT_TOKEN)\nbot.start((ctx) => ctx.reply('Welcome'))\nbot.help((ctx) => ctx.reply('Send me a sticker'))\nbot.on(message('sticker'), (ctx) => ctx.reply('👍'))\nbot.hears('hi', (ctx) => ctx.reply('Hey there'))\n\nbot.launch()\n\n// Enable graceful stop\nprocess.once('SIGINT', () => bot.stop('SIGINT'))\nprocess.once('SIGTERM', () => bot.stop('SIGTERM'))\n```\n\n### With Fastify Integration\n\nFor more advanced setups using Fastify, you can close both the Fastify server and the bot together in the signal handlers.\n\n```javascript\nprocess.once('SIGINT', () => {\n  fastify.close();\n  fastify.bot.stop('SIGINT');\n});\nprocess.once('SIGTERM', () => {\n  fastify.close();\n  fastify.bot.stop('SIGTERM');\n});\n```\n\n### Known Issue with Graceful Shutdown\n\nThere was a reported issue where implementing graceful shutdown could cause updates to be handled twice when SIGINT is sent while the bot is processing an update, and then restarted.\n\nThe current version of Telegraf is v4.16.3.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."

      "Based on the search results, here's the information about Telegraf `bot.stop()` graceful shutdown with SIGINT in Node.js:\n\n## Graceful Shutdown in Telegraf\n\nTelegraf is a library that makes it simple for you to develop your own Telegram bots using JavaScript or TypeScript.\n\n### Standard Pattern for Graceful Shutdown\n\nOn NodeJS, to make sure connections are gracefully closed before process is killed, you can use the process.once hooks. This is safe to add to the end of your bot's entry file.\n\nThe recommended pattern is:\n\n```javascript\nprocess.once('SIGINT', () => bot.stop('SIGINT'));\nprocess.once('SIGTERM', () => bot.stop('SIGTERM'));\n```\n\n### Complete Example\n\nThe official documentation shows the pattern with `bot.launch()` followed by the graceful stop handlers for both SIGINT and SIGTERM signals.\n\n```javascript\nconst { Telegraf } = require('telegraf')\nconst { message } = require('telegraf/filters')\n\nconst bot = new Telegraf(process.env.BOT_TOKEN)\nbot.start((ctx) => ctx.reply('Welcome'))\nbot.help((ctx) => ctx.reply('Send me a sticker'))\nbot.on(message('sticker'), (ctx) => ctx.reply('👍'))\nbot.hears('hi', (ctx) => ctx.reply('Hey there'))\n\nbot.launch()\n\n// Enable graceful stop\nprocess.once('SIGINT', () => bot.stop('SIGINT'))\nprocess.once('SIGTERM', () => bot.stop('SIGTERM'))\n```\n\n### With Fastify Integration\n\nFor more advanced setups using Fastify, you can close both the Fastify server and the bot together in the signal handlers.\n\n```javascript\nprocess.once('SIGINT', () => {\n  fastify.close();\n  fastify.bot.stop('SIGINT');\n});\nprocess.once('SIGTERM', () => {\n  fastify.close();\n  fastify.bot.stop('SIGTERM');\n});\n```\n\n### Known Issue with Graceful Shutdown\n\nThere was a reported issue where implementing graceful shutdown could cause updates to be handled twice when SIGINT is sent while the bot is processing an update, and then restarted.\n\nThe current version of Telegraf is v4.16.3."

If one of these signals has a listener installed, its default behavior will be removed (Node.js will no longer exit).\n\nThis is the fundamental issue - as noted in documentation, Node does not exit when you assign a listener to SIGINT, you must call `process.exit()` if you want to exit.\n\n### Event Loop Blocking Issue\n\nWhen you are inside a while loop, the SIGINT event isn't triggered, resulting in the script trapped in the loop with no nice way of stopping.(no content)A solution is to change the while loop to use `setInterval` instead: `var interval = setInterval(function () { /* Your program */ }, 0);` - this way, when the user hits CTRL+C the event handler executes as expected.\n\n### Recent 2025 Issue with Libraries\n\nA recent issue (July 2025) with openai-agents-js shows that some libraries register global SIGINT/SIGTERM signal handlers that internally call `process.exit()`, which immediately ends the process without allowing time for user-defined signal handlers to run.(no content)Simply importing such a library breaks user-defined SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers - for example, handlers that run custom logic to gracefully shut down other parts of an application (waiting for in-progress HTTP requests to finish, closing long-lived resources, etc.), because the signal handlers installed by the library end up calling `process.exit()` before the user-defined signal handlers have a chance to complete.\n\n### Best Practices for Graceful Shutdown\n\nA recommended pattern is to use a centralized exit handler that handles multiple signals and uses `process.nextTick()` to ensure proper cleanup.(no content)Listening to events like SIGINT or SIGTERM can help you implement graceful shutdowns, a critical aspect of building resilient applications.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."

· Issue #836 · telegraf/telegraf\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/telegraf/telegraf/issues/836\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about Telegraf's `bot.stop()` method, promises, async/await, and graceful shutdown with polling:\n\n## Graceful Shutdown Pattern\n\nThe official Telegraf documentation shows the recommended pattern for graceful shutdown using signal handlers:\n\n```javascript\nbot.launch();\n\n// Enable graceful stop\nprocess.once('SIGINT', () => bot.stop('SIGINT'));\nprocess.once('SIGTERM', () => bot.stop('SIGTERM'));\n```\n\n## Key Points About `bot.stop()`\n\n1. **The `bot.stop()` method** accepts an optional reason string (like `'SIGINT'` or `'SIGTERM'`) and is used to gracefully stop the bot.\n\n2. \"Doing otherwise is kinda ignoring the problem: bot still cannot be stopped gracefully because middleware haven't finished.\" This indicates that graceful shutdown waits for middleware to complete.\n\n3. If middleware isn't awaited before fetching updates, it breaks \"any semblance of sensible update confirming\" and error handling, causing every error to turn into an unhandled rejection.\n\n## Polling Behavior and Handler Timeout\n\nIn the polling rewrite, the default behavior was changed to resolve the promise if processing takes longer than 5 seconds. However, this means \"errors thrown after 5 seconds are swallowed.\"(no content)The polling loop in `telegraf/src/core/network/polling.ts` uses an async iterator pattern where it awaits `Promise.all(updates.map(handleUpdate))` for each batch of updates.\n\n## Awaiting `bot.launch()`\n\n\"Trying to await for the bot to launch stops the code. I think this is done so that the code does not close after the bot is launched.\" This is intentional behavior - it worked differently in older versions of Telegraf (4.10.0).\n\n## Error Handling\n\nBy default, Telegraf will print all errors to stderr and rethrow the error.(no content)If middleware throws an error or times out, Telegraf calls `bot.handleError`, and you can overwrite it using `bot.catch` if needed.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."

The loop structure is:\n\n```typescript\ndo {\n  try {\n    const updates = await this.telegram.callApi(...)\n    // process updates\n  } catch (error) {\n    // handle errors\n  }\n} while (!this.abortController.signal.aborted)\n```\n\nWhen `stop()` executes `abort()`, it sets the signal's `aborted` property to true. This causes:\n\n1. **Immediate exit**: The `while` condition evaluates to true on the next iteration, terminating the loop\n2. **AbortError handling**: Any pending API calls receive an `AbortError`, caught and returned early\n3. **Cleanup**: The calling code in the `loop()` method triggers a `finally` block that invokes `syncUpdateOffset()` before stopping\n\nThe design is clean: a synchronous trigger initiates graceful async shutdown."

Configuration options\n    25→ * @param {boolean} options.noSentry - Disable Sentry if true\n    26→ * @param {boolean} options.debug - Enable debug mode\n    27→ * @param {string} options.environment - Environment name (production, development, etc)\n    28→ */\n    29→export const initializeSentry = async (options = {}) => {\n    30→  // Check if --no-sentry flag is present\n    31→  if (options.noSentry || process.argv.includes('--no-sentry')) {\n    32→    sentryDisabled = true;\n    33→    if (options.debug) {\n    34→      console.log('ℹ️  Sentry is disabled via --no-sentry flag');\n    35→    }\n    36→    return;\n    37→  }\n    38→\n    39→  // Sentry is already initialized in instrument.mjs\n    40→  // This function is for additional runtime configuration\n    41→  if (isSentryEnabled()) {\n    42→    const sentry = await getSentry();\n    43→    if (sentry) {\n    44→      // Set user context if available\n    45→      if (process.env.USER || process.env.USERNAME) {\n    46→        sentry.setUser({\n    47→          username: process.env.USER || process.env.USERNAME,\n    48→        });\n    49→      }\n    50→\n    51→      // Set additional tags\n    52→      sentry.setTags({\n    53→        node_version: process.version,\n    54→        platform: process.platform,\n    55→        arch: process.arch,\n    56→        hive_mind_version: options.version || process.env.npm_package_version || 'unknown',\n    57→      });\n    58→\n    59→      if (options.debug) {\n    60→        console.log('✅ Sentry integration configured');\n    61→      }\n    62→    }\n    63→  }\n    64→};\n    65→\n    66→/**\n    67→ * Wrap a function with Sentry error tracking\n    68→ * @param {Function} fn - Function to wrap\n    69→ * @param {string} name - Name of the operation\n    70→ * @param {string} op - Operation type (default: 'task')\n    71→ * @returns {Function} Wrapped function\n    72→ */\n    73→export const withSentry = (fn, name, op = 'task') => {\n    74→  return async (...args) => {\n    75→    if (!isSentryEnabled() || sentryDisabled) {\n    76→      return fn(...args);\n    77→    }\n    78→\n    79→    const transaction = startTransaction(name, op);\n    80→\n    81→    try {\n    82→      const result = await fn(...args);\n    83→      transaction.setStatus('ok');\n    84→      return result;\n    85→    } catch (error) {\n    86→      transaction.setStatus('internal_error');\n    87→      captureException(error, {\n    88→        operation: name,\n    89→        args: args.length > 0 ? `${args.length} arguments` : 'no arguments',\n    90→      });\n    91→      throw error;\n    92→    } finally {\n    93→      // In Sentry v10, use end() instead of finish()\n    94→      if (transaction.end) {\n    95→        transaction.end();\n    96→      } else if (transaction.finish) {\n    97→        // Fallback for compatibility\n    98→        transaction.finish();\n    99→      }\n   100→    }\n   101→  };\n   102→};\n   103→\n   104→/**\n   105→ * Create a Sentry span for tracking a specific operation\n   106→ * @param {string} name - Configuration options\n * @param {boolean} options.noSentry - Disable Sentry if true\n * @param {boolean} options.debug - Enable debug mode\n * @param {string} options.environment - Environment name (production, development, etc)\n */\nexport const initializeSentry = async (options = {}) => {\n  // Check if --no-sentry flag is present\n  if (options.noSentry || process.argv.includes('--no-sentry')) {\n    sentryDisabled = true;\n    if (options.debug) {\n      console.log('ℹ️  Sentry is disabled via --no-sentry flag');\n    }\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Sentry is already initialized in instrument.mjs\n  // This function is for additional runtime configuration\n  if (isSentryEnabled()) {\n    const sentry = await getSentry();\n    if (sentry) {\n      // Set user context if available\n      if (process.env.USER || process.env.USERNAME) {\n        sentry.setUser({\n          username: process.env.USER || process.env.USERNAME,\n        });\n      }\n\n      // Set additional tags\n      sentry.setTags({\n        node_version: process.version,\n        platform: process.platform,\n        arch: process.arch,\n        hive_mind_version: options.version || process.env.npm_package_version || 'unknown',\n      });\n\n      if (options.debug) {\n        console.log('✅ Sentry integration configured');\n      }\n    }\n  }\n};\n\n/**\n * Wrap a function with Sentry error tracking\n * @param {Function} fn - Function to wrap\n * @param {string} name - Name of the operation\n * @param {string} op - Operation type (default: 'task')\n * @returns {Function} Wrapped function\n */\nexport const withSentry = (fn, name, op = 'task') => {\n  return async (...args) => {\n    if (!isSentryEnabled() || sentryDisabled) {\n      return fn(...args);\n    }\n\n    const transaction = startTransaction(name, op);\n\n    try {\n      const result = await fn(...args);\n      transaction.setStatus('ok');\n      return result;\n    } catch (error) {\n      transaction.setStatus('internal_error');\n      captureException(error, {\n        operation: name,\n        args: args.length > 0 ? `${args.length} arguments` : 'no arguments',\n      });\n      throw error;\n    } finally {\n      // In Sentry v10, use end() instead of finish()\n      if (transaction.end) {\n        transaction.end();\n      } else if (transaction.finish) {\n        // Fallback for compatibility\n        transaction.finish();\n      }\n    }\n  };\n};\n\n/**\n * Create a Sentry span for tracking a specific operation\n * @param {string} name - Name of the span\n * @param {Function} callback - Callback to execute within the span\n * @returns {Promise} Result of the callback\n */\nexport const withSpan = async (name, callback) => {\n  if (!isSentryEnabled() || sentryDisabled) {\n    return callback();\n  }\n\n  const sentry = await getSentry();\n  if (!sentry) {\n    return callback();\n  }\n\n  return sentry.startSpan(\n    {\n      name,\n      op: 'function',\n    },\n    async () => {\n      return callback();\n    }\n  );\n};\n\n/**\n * Log a message to Sentry\n * @param {string} message - Message to log\n * @param {string} level - Log level (debug, info, warning, error, fatal)\n * @param {Object} context - Additional context\n */\nexport const logToSentry = (message, level = 'info', context = {}) => {\n  if (!isSentryEnabled() || sentryDisabled) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  captureMessage(message, level, context);\n};\n\n/**\n * Report an error to Sentry\n * Use this for actual errors that indicate something went wrong\n * @param {Error} error - Error to capture\n * @param {Object} context - Additional context\n */\nexport const reportError = (error, context = {}) => {\n  if (!isSentryEnabled() || sentryDisabled) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  captureException(error, { ...context, level: 'error' });\n};\n\n/**\n * Report a warning to Sentry\n * Use this for non-critical issues that should be tracked but don't indicate failure\n * @param {string|Error} warning - Warning message or error object\n * @param {Object} context - Additional context\n */\nexport const reportWarning = (warning, context = {}) => {\n  if (!isSentryEnabled() || sentryDisabled) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Convert string warnings to Error objects for better stack traces\n  const warningError = typeof warning === 'string' ? new Error(warning) : warning;\n  captureException(warningError, { ...context, level: 'warning' });\n};\n\n/**\n * Add breadcrumb for better error context\n * @param {Object} breadcrumb - Breadcrumb data\n */\nexport const addBreadcrumb = async breadcrumb => {\n  if (!isSentryEnabled() || sentryDisabled) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const sentry = await getSentry();\n  if (sentry) {\n    sentry.addBreadcrumb(breadcrumb);\n  }\n};\n\n/**\n * Set user context for Sentry\n * @param {Object} user - User data\n */\nexport const setUserContext = async user => {\n  if (!isSentryEnabled() || sentryDisabled) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const sentry = await getSentry();\n  if (sentry) {\n    sentry.setUser(user);\n  }\n};\n\n/**\n * Set extra context for Sentry\n * @param {string} key - Context key\n * @param {any} value - Context value\n */\nexport const setExtraContext = async (key, value) => {\n  if (!isSentryEnabled() || sentryDisabled) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const sentry = await getSentry();\n  if (sentry) {\n    sentry.setExtra(key, value);\n  }\n};\n\n/**\n * Set tags for Sentry\n * @param {Object} tags - Tags to set\n */\nexport const setTags = async tags => {\n  if (!isSentryEnabled() || sentryDisabled) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const sentry = await getSentry();\n  if (sentry) {\n    sentry.setTags(tags);\n  }\n};\n\n/**\n * Flush Sentry events before exit\n * @param {number} timeout - Timeout in milliseconds (default: 2000)\n * @returns {Promise} Promise that resolves when flush is complete\n */\nexport const flushSentry = async (timeout = 2000) => {\n  if (!isSentryEnabled() || sentryDisabled) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const sentry = await getSentry();\n  if (!sentry) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  try {\n    await sentry.flush(timeout);\n  } catch (error) {\n    // Silently fail if flush fails\n    if (process.env.DEBUG === 'true') {\n      console.error('Failed to flush Sentry events:', error.message);\n    }\n  }\n};\n\n/**\n * Close Sentry client\n * @param {number} timeout - Timeout in milliseconds (default: 2000)\n * @returns {Promise} Promise that resolves when close is complete\n */\nexport const closeSentry = async (timeout = 2000) => {\n  if (!isSentryEnabled() || sentryDisabled) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const sentry = await getSentry();\n  if (!sentry) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  try {\n    await sentry.close(timeout);\n  } catch (error) {\n    // Silently fail if close fails\n    if (process.env.DEBUG === 'true') {\n      console.error('Failed to close Sentry:', error.message);\n    }\n  }\n};\n\n// Export the Sentry check function\nexport { isSentryEnabled };\n","numLines":288,"startLine":1,"totalLines":288}}}

[2026-01-09T23:57:39.730Z] [INFO] {
  "type": "assistant",
  "message": {
    "model": "claude-opus-4-5-20251101",
    "id": "msg_01Pcz6XBV9SsrRQavZUMEQ7h",
    "type": "message",
    "role": "assistant",
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "tool_use",
        "id": "toolu_014KcwZjWzZuto6u7E4QcBWM",
        "name": "Write",
        "input": {
          "file_path": "/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1768002786367/docs/case-studies/issue-1083/README.md",
          "content": "# Case Study: Issue #1083 - Slow SIGINT Shutdown in hive-telegram-bot\n\n## Issue Summary\n\n**Issue:** [#1083](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1083)\n**Title:** Something taking too long to shutdown on CTRL+C of hive-telegram-bot\n**Reporter:** konard\n**Type:** Bug\n\n**Symptom:** When pressing Ctrl+C to stop `hive-telegram-bot`, the bot displays \"Received SIGINT (Ctrl+C), stopping bot...\" but then hangs. Users must press Ctrl+C twice to exit.\n\n## Evidence\n\n### Screenshot Analysis\n![Screenshot](screenshot.png)\n\nThe screenshot shows:\n1. Bot receives `/limits` and `/solve-queue` commands working normally\n2. User presses Ctrl+C\n3. Bot prints `Received SIGINT (Ctrl+C), stopping bot...`\n4. VERBOSE output shows: Signal: SIGINT, Process ID: 1850, Parent Process ID: 1508\n5. Process hangs (cursor blinking, no exit)\n\n### Log Evidence from Issue Description\n```\n[VERBOSE] /solve-queue found 0 running claude processes\n^C\nReceived SIGINT (Ctrl+C), stopping bot...\n[VERBOSE] Signal: SIGINT\n[VERBOSE] Process ID: 1850\n[VERBOSE] Parent Process ID: 1508\n```\n\n## Timeline / Sequence of Events\n\n### Expected Flow\n1. User presses Ctrl+C\n2. Process receives SIGINT\n3. Signal handler calls `bot.stop('SIGINT')`\n4. Telegraf stops polling loop\n5. Event loop empties\n6. Process exits\n\n### Actual Flow (Bug)\n1. User presses Ctrl+C\n2. Process receives SIGINT\n3. Signal handler calls `bot.stop('SIGINT')` (synchronous)\n4. Telegraf sets `abortController.signal.aborted = true`\n5. **BUG: SolveQueue consumer loop continues running (not stopped)**\n6. SolveQueue's `sleep()` keeps the event loop alive\n7. Process hangs waiting for event loop to empty\n8. User presses Ctrl+C again\n9. Second SIGINT triggers default handler behavior\n10. Process force-exits\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\n\n### Primary Root Cause\n\n**The SolveQueue consumer loop is not stopped during shutdown.**\n\nIn `telegram-bot.mjs` lines 1468-1500, the SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers only call `bot.stop()`:\n\n```javascript\nprocess.once('SIGINT', () => {\n  isShuttingDown = true;\n  console.log('\\n Received SIGINT (Ctrl+C), stopping bot...');\n  // ... verbose logging ...\n  bot.stop('SIGINT');  // Only stops Telegraf, not the queue\n});\n```\n\nHowever, the `SolveQueue` class in `telegram-solve-queue.lib.mjs` has its own consumer loop:\n\n```javascript\nasync runConsumer() {\n  this.log('Consumer started');\n\n  while (this.isRunning) {       // <-- This never becomes false\n    if (this.queue.length === 0) {\n      await this.sleep(QUEUE_CONFIG.CONSUMER_POLL_INTERVAL_MS);  // <-- Keeps event loop alive\n      continue;\n    }\n    // ... process queue items ...\n  }\n\n  this.log('Consumer stopped');\n  this.consumerTask = null;\n}\n```\n\nThe `sleep()` method creates a `setTimeout` promise that keeps Node.js's event loop active:\n\n```javascript\nsleep(ms) {\n  return new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, ms));\n}\n```\n\n### Why the Second Ctrl+C Works\n\nThe first SIGINT handler is registered with `process.once('SIGINT', ...)`, so it only fires once. When the user presses Ctrl+C a second time:\n\n1. There's no custom SIGINT handler anymore\n2. Node.js reverts to default SIGINT behavior\n3. Default behavior terminates the process immediately\n\n### Contributing Factors\n\n1. **Missing queue shutdown:** The SIGINT handler doesn't call `solveQueue.stop()`\n2. **Async consumer loop:** The consumer loop runs indefinitely until `isRunning = false`\n3. **Non-ref'd timers:** The `setTimeout` in `sleep()` uses regular timers, not unref'd timers\n4. **No cleanup coordination:** No mechanism to coordinate cleanup between Telegraf and the queue\n\n## Proposed Solutions\n\n### Solution 1: Stop the Queue on Shutdown (Recommended)\n\nAdd queue shutdown to the signal handlers:\n\n```javascript\nprocess.once('SIGINT', () => {\n  isShuttingDown = true;\n  console.log('\\n Received SIGINT (Ctrl+C), stopping bot...');\n  if (VERBOSE) {\n    console.log('[VERBOSE] Signal: SIGINT');\n    console.log('[VERBOSE] Process ID:', process.pid);\n    console.log('[VERBOSE] Parent Process ID:', process.ppid);\n  }\n\n  // Stop the solve queue consumer loop\n  const solveQueue = getSolveQueue({ verbose: VERBOSE });\n  solveQueue.stop();\n\n  bot.stop('SIGINT');\n});\n```\n\n**Pros:**\n- Minimal code change\n- Proper cleanup of queue resources\n- Coordinated shutdown\n\n**Cons:**\n- Need to handle case where queue hasn't been initialized yet\n\n### Solution 2: Use Unref'd Timers in Queue\n\nModify the `sleep()` method to use unref'd timers:\n\n```javascript\nsleep(ms) {\n  return new Promise(resolve => {\n    const timer = setTimeout(resolve, ms);\n    timer.unref();  // Don't keep event loop alive\n  });\n}\n```\n\n**Pros:**\n- Event loop can exit naturally\n- No explicit stop() call needed\n\n**Cons:**\n- Changes behavior of queue (may exit prematurely in edge cases)\n- Doesn't properly stop queue processing\n\n### Solution 3: Centralized Exit Handler\n\nCreate a centralized shutdown coordinator:\n\n```javascript\nconst shutdownManager = {\n  handlers: [],\n  register(handler) { this.handlers.push(handler); },\n  async shutdown(signal) {\n    for (const handler of this.handlers) {\n      await handler(signal);\n    }\n    process.exit(0);\n  }\n};\n\n// Register handlers\nshutdownManager.register(async () => solveQueue.stop());\nshutdownManager.register(async () => bot.stop('SIGINT'));\n\nprocess.once('SIGINT', () => shutdownManager.shutdown('SIGINT'));\n```\n\n**Pros:**\n- Clean architecture\n- Extensible for future components\n- Proper async handling\n\n**Cons:**\n- More code\n- Over-engineering for current needs\n\n## Recommended Implementation\n\n**Implement Solution 1** with a safety check:\n\n```javascript\nprocess.once('SIGINT', () => {\n  isShuttingDown = true;\n  console.log('\\n Received SIGINT (Ctrl+C), stopping bot...');\n  if (VERBOSE) {\n    console.log('[VERBOSE] Signal: SIGINT');\n    console.log('[VERBOSE] Process ID:', process.pid);\n    console.log('[VERBOSE] Parent Process ID:', process.ppid);\n  }\n\n  // Stop the solve queue consumer loop to allow clean exit\n  try {\n    const solveQueue = getSolveQueue({ verbose: VERBOSE });\n    solveQueue.stop();\n    if (VERBOSE) {\n      console.log('[VERBOSE] Solve queue stopped');\n    }\n  } catch (err) {\n    // Queue may not be initialized, ignore\n    if (VERBOSE) {\n      console.log('[VERBOSE] Could not stop solve queue:', err.message);\n    }\n  }\n\n  bot.stop('SIGINT');\n});\n```\n\nApply the same change to the SIGTERM handler.\n\n## Additional Recommendations\n\n1. **Add shutdown logging:** After `bot.stop()`, add a log to confirm shutdown started\n2. **Consider timeout:** Add a fallback timeout to force exit if graceful shutdown takes too long\n3. **Document behavior:** Add comments explaining the shutdown sequence\n\n## References\n\n- [Telegraf Documentation](https://telegraf.js.org/) - Official Telegraf documentation\n- [Node.js Process Events](https://nodejs.org/api/process.html) - Node.js signal handling\n- [Telegraf GitHub](https://github.com/telegraf/telegraf) - Telegraf source code\n- [Node.js SIGINT Handling Issue](https://github.com/nodejs/node/issues/2853) - Related Node.js issue\n- [Node.js Event Loop Blocking](https://github.com/nodejs/node/issues/9050) - SIGINT and loops issue\n\n## Test Plan\n\n1. Start `hive-telegram-bot --verbose`\n2. Wait for bot to fully initialize\n3. Send `/limits` command to ensure queue is active\n4. Press Ctrl+C once\n5. Verify bot exits cleanly without hanging\n6. Check that \"Solve queue stopped\" message appears in verbose mode\n"
        }
      }
